sumBat = (loads) => loads.reduce((s,r)=>s+toPbat(r.a,r.v),0);\nconst hTot = sumBat(HOVER_LOADS), cTot = sumBat(CRUISE_LOADS);\nconst hMin = (BAT_CAP*0.8/hTot*60).toFixed(1);\nconst cMin = (BAT_CAP*0.8/cTot*60).toFixed(1);\nconst h3Min= (3.0*0.8/hTot*60).toFixed(1);\nconst c3Min= (3.0*0.8/cTot*60).toFixed(1);\n\n// \u2500\u2500 primitives xp(360);\n\n// Hull profile (same as before)\nconst HULL_PROFILE=[\n  [0,0],[8,10],[22,18],[40,30],[58,36],[88,37],\n  [120,42],[140,42],[165,40],[190,36],[220,29],\n sumBat = (loads) => loads.reduce((s,r)=>s+toPbat(r.a,r.v),0);\nconst hTot = sumBat(HOVER_LOADS), cTot = sumBat(CRUISE_LOADS);\nconst hMin = (BAT_CAP*0.8/hTot*60).toFixed(1);\nconst cMin = (BAT_CAP*0.8/cTot*60).toFixed(1);\nconst h3Min= (3.0*0.8/hTot*60).toFixed(1);\nconst c3Min= (3.0*0.8/cTot*60).toFixed(1);\n\n// \u2500\u2500 primitives xp(360);\n\n// Hull profile (same as before)\nconst HULL_PROFILE=[\n  [0,0],[8,10],[22,18],[40,30],[58,36],[88,37],\n  [120,42],[140,42],[165,40],[190,36],[220,29],\n It also means the transition to cruise can begin at a slightly lower altitude since there's more margin to catch any altitude loss.\n\n**ESC upgrade to 35A:** The 65mm draws 28A at WOT, so 30A ESCs were marginal. At hover the ESCs sit at only ~30% of their 35A rating \u2014 they'll barely warm up. The Power tab shows the full ESC thermal budget across hover, transition, and WOT.\n\n**Battery recommendation:** The 5S 2200mAh still works (~5.4 min hover), but the 5S 3000mAh brings it to ~7.4 min hover and ~8.2 min cruise at only +70g weight \u2014 T/W stays at a healthy 1.99:1. Worth it for any mission beyond a quick functional test.",

    <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.dim,fontSize:10}}>{b.brand}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.orange}}>{b.mass}g</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:b.twOk?C.green:C.red,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>{b.tw}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:b.twOk?C.teal:C.dimmer}}>{b.twOk?b.crsMin+\" min\":\"\u2014\"}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\"}}>\n                {!b.twOk?<span style={{color:C.red,fontSize:10}}>T/W {\"<\"} 2.0 \u2717</span>\n                 :b===best?<span style={{color:C.green,fontWeight:\"bold\",fontSize:10}}>\u2605 RECOMMENDED</span>\n                 :<span style={{color:C.dim,fontSize:10}}>OK</span>}\n              </td>\n            </tr>\n          ))}</tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      {best && (<>\n        <SH t={`Recommended: ${best.name}`} c={C.green}/>\n        <div style={{display:\"grid\",gridTemplateColumns:\"1fr 1fr\",gap:24}}>\n          <div>\n            <KV k=\"Capacity\"         v={`${best.cap} mAh`}/>\n            <KV k=\"Cell count\"       v=\"5S (18.5V nominal)\"/>\n            <KV k=\"C-rating\"         v={`${best.c}C continuous`}/>\n            <KV k=\"Max continuous\"   v={`${best.maxDraw}A`} vc={C.green}/>\n            <KV k=\"Mass\"             v={`${best.mass}g`}/>\n            <KV k=\"AUW with battery\" v={`${best.auw}g`}/>\n            <KV k=\"T/W ratio\"        v={`${best.tw}:1`} vc={C.green}/>\n            <KV k=\"Hover endurance\"  v={`${best.hovrMin} min`} vc={C.yellow}/>\n            <KV k=\"Cruise endurance\" v={`${best.crsMin} min`} vc={C.teal}/>\n            <KV k=\"Connector\"        v=\"XT60 (crimp 12AWG silicone to power bus)\"/>\n            <KV k=\"Dimensions (typ)\" v=\"114 \u00d7 35 \u00d7 29 mm\"/>\n          </div>\n          <div>\n            <Note c={C.green} ch={`${best.name} delivers the best endurance of any battery that keeps T/W \u2265 2.0. At ${best.tw}:1 T/W there is comfortable margin for wind gusts, transition manoeuvres, and up to ~120g of payload without dropping below 2.0.`}/>\n            <Warn ch={`30C continuous batteries require careful in-flight monitoring. At peak draw (~70A all fans WOT) a 30C/${best.cap}mAh pack delivers ${best.maxDraw}A \u2014 just at the limit. Use 45C or higher for aggressive manoeuvres. Tattu Funfly or Gens Ace G-Tech in ${best.cap}mAh 45C are preferred.`}/>\n            <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"Battery slide rail range: \u00b122mm on 2\u00d7 M3 aluminium keel rails. With the heavier battery at this mass, the centroid sits at ~197mm from nose \u2014 within the \u00b122mm trim window. No ballast required.\"/>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </>)}\n\n      <SH t=\"Endurance vs Battery Mass (T/W \u2265 2.0 window)\"/>\n      {/* Inline bar chart */}\n      <div style={{display:\"flex\",flexDirection:\"column\",gap:8,marginTop:8}}>\n        {BATTERIES.filter(b=>b.twOk).map(b=>{\n          const maxH=Math.max(...BATTERIES.filter(x=>x.twOk).map(x=>parseFloat(x.hovrMin)));\n          return(\n            <div key={b.id} style={{display:\"flex\",alignItems:\"center\",gap:10}}>\n              <span style={{color:C.yellow,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:22}}>{b.id}</span>\n              <span style={{color:C.dim,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:165,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{b.name}</span>\n              <div style={{flex:1,background:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.05)\",borderRadius:2,height:14,position:\"relative\"}}>\n                <div style={{width:`${parseFloat(b.hovrMin)/maxH*100}%`,height:\"100%\",\n                  background:b===best?C.green:C.teal,opacity:.7,borderRadius:2}}/>\n              </div>\n              <span style={{color:b===best?C.green:C.teal,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:55}}>{b.hovrMin} min</span>\n              <span style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,minWidth:50}}>T/W {b.tw}</span>\n            </div>\n          );\n        })}\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n// NAVIGATION LIGHTS TAB\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\nfunction NavLightDiagram(){\n  const VW=720, VH=360, CY=180;\n  const SC=(mm)=>mm*1.28, NX=55, xp=(mm)=>NX+SC(mm);\n\n  // hull outline top view\n  const PROF=[[0,0],[8,10],[22,18],[40,30],[58,36],[88,37],[120,42],[140,42],[165,40],[190,36],[220,29],[252,18],[260,16],[278,24],[305,29],[330,28],[360,22]];\n  const upper=PROF.map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY-SC(y)]);\n  const lower=[...PROF].reverse().map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY+SC(y)]);\n  const outline=[...upper,...lower].map((p,i)=>i===0?`M${p[0]},${p[1]}`:`L${p[0]},${p[1]}`).join(\" \")+\"Z\";\n\n  // arc helpers for coverage arcs\n  function arc(cx,cy,r,startDeg,endDeg,color,dash=\"\"){\n    const s=startDeg*Math.PI/180, e=endDeg*Math.PI/180;\n    const x1=cx+r*Math.cos(s), y1=cy+r*Math.sin(s);\n    const x2=cx+r*Math.cos(e), y2=cy+r*Math.sin(e);\n    const large=(endDeg-startDeg)>180?1:0;\n    return <path d={`M${cx},${cy} L${x1},${y1} A${r},${r} 0 ${large},1 ${x2},${y2} Z`}\n      fill={`${color}18`} stroke={color} strokeWidth={0.8} strokeDasharray={dash||\"\"} opacity={0.8}/>;\n  }\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n      {/* Coverage arcs \u2014 top view */}\n      {/* Port red: 110\u00b0 arc centred on left wing tip, facing forward-left */}\n      {arc(xp(155), CY-SC(340), SC(55), -145, -35, \"#ff2020\")}\n      {/* Stbd green: 110\u00b0 arc centred on right wing tip, facing forward-right */}\n      {arc(xp(155), CY+SC(340), SC(55), 35, 145, \"#00dd00\")}\n      {/* Tail white: 140\u00b0 arc, aft */}\n      {arc(xp(350), CY, SC(55), -70, 70, \"#ffffff\")}\n      {/* Anti-collision 360\u00b0 */}\n      <circle cx={xp(120)} cy={CY} r={SC(40)}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,68,68,0.05)\" stroke=\"#ff4444\" strokeWidth={0.8} strokeDasharray=\"4 3\" opacity={0.7}/>\n      {/* Belly strobe 360\u00b0 */}\n      <circle cx={xp(160)} cy={CY} r={SC(35)}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.03)\" stroke=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.3)\" strokeWidth={0.7} strokeDasharray=\"3 3\"/>\n\n      {/* Hull */}\n      <path d={outline} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.06)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      {/* Arms */}\n      {[-1,1].map(side=>(\n        <g key={side}>\n          <line x1={xp(128)} y1={CY+side*SC(50)} x2={xp(160)} y2={CY+side*SC(340)}\n            stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n          {/* Nacelle */}\n          <ellipse cx={xp(155)} cy={CY+side*SC(340)} rx={SC(34)} ry={SC(14)}\n            fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.05)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.2}/>\n        </g>\n      ))}\n\n      {/* Port LED */}\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY-SC(340)} r={7} fill=\"#ff2020\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY-SC(340)} r={14} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#ff2020\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(140)-18} y={CY-SC(340)-16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ff2020\" fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">PORT \u25cf</text>\n\n      {/* Stbd LED */}\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY+SC(340)} r={7} fill=\"#00dd00\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY+SC(340)} r={14} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#00dd00\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(140)+18} y={CY+SC(340)+20} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#00dd00\" fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">STBD \u25cf</text>\n\n      {/* Tail white */}\n      <circle cx={xp(350)} cy={CY} r={7} fill=\"#ffffff\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(350)} cy={CY} r={14} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#ffffff\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(350)+18} y={CY-4} fill=\"#dddddd\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>TAIL \u25cf</text>\n\n      {/* Anti-collision dorsal */}\n      <circle cx={xp(120)} cy={CY} r={7} fill=\"#ff4444\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <text x={xp(120)} y={CY-14} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ff4444\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">ACOL\u25c6</text>\n\n      {/* Belly strobe */}\n      <circle cx={xp(160)} cy={CY} r={5} fill=\"rgba(255,255,200,0.8)\" stroke=\"#ffffaa\" strokeWidth={0.8}/>\n      <text x={xp(160)} y={CY+16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ffffaa\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>BELLY\u25c6</text>\n\n      {/* Landing light */}\n      <rect x={xp(22)} y={CY-SC(8)} width={SC(16)} height={SC(16)} rx={2}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,255,120,0.2)\" stroke=\"#ffff80\" strokeWidth={1}/>\n      <text x={xp(30)} y={CY-SC(13)} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ffff80\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>LAND</text>\n\n      {/* Nose / tail labels */}\n      <text x={NX-8} y={CY+4} textAnchor=\"end\" fill={C.dimmer} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>NOSE</text>\n      <text x={xp(360)+6} y={CY+4} fill={C.dimmer} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>TAIL</text>\n      <text x={VW/2} y={16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.25)\"\n        fontSize={9} fontFamily={M} letterSpacing=\"0.12em\">NAVIGATION LIGHT LAYOUT \u2014 TOP VIEW \u2014 COVERAGE ARCS SHOWN</text>\n\n      {/* Legend */}\n      {[[\"#ff2020\",\"Port red (steady)\"],[\"#00dd00\",\"Stbd green (steady)\"],\n        [\"#ffffff\",\"Tail white (steady)\"],[\"#ff4444\",\"Anti-col dorsal (strobe)\"],\n        [\"#ffffaa\",\"Belly strobe\"],[\"#ffff80\",\"Landing/hover light\"]].map(([col,lbl],i)=>(\n        <g key={i}>\n          <circle cx={12} cy={240+i*18} r={5} fill={col} opacity={0.9}/>\n          <text x={22} y={244+i*18} fill={col} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M}>{lbl}</text>\n        </g>\n      ))}\n    </svg>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction NavLightsTab(){\n  return(\n    <div>\n      <div style={{background:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.03)\",border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,\n        padding:\"10px 14px\",marginBottom:16,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n        Navigation lights comply with <span style={{color:C.accent}}>ICAO Annex 2</span>,&nbsp;\n        <span style={{color:C.orange}}>14 CFR Part 91.209</span>, and&nbsp;\n        <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>FAA AC 107-2B</span> (sUAS night operations).\n        All LEDs are WS2812C-2020 individually addressable RGB, driven by Pico 2 PIO state machine on GP26.\n      </div>\n\n      <div style={{border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,background:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.01)\",padding:8,marginBottom:20}}>\n        <NavLightDiagram/>\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"Light style={{background:i%2===0?\"rgba(0,229,255,0.025)\":\"transparent\",verticalAlign:\"top\"}}>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.yellow,fontSize:10,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{l.lumen}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.dimmer,fontSize:9}}>{l.regs.split(\"\u00b7\")[0]}</td>\n            </tr>\n          ))}</tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"LED Controller Spec\"/>\n      <div style={{display:\"grid\",gridTemplateColumns:\"1fr 1fr\",gap:24}}>\n        <div>\n          <KV k=\"LED type\" v=\"WS2812C-2020 (2020 package, 5V)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Chain length\" v=\"6 LEDs on a single data line\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Data pin\" v=\"Pico 2 GP26 \u2192 PIO0 state machine\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Protocol\" v=\"800kHz NZR (WS2812 standard)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Power\" v=\"5V from BEC \u00b7 max 60mA per LED at white\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Total LED current\" v=\"~180mA max (all white full brightness)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Anti-collision rate\" v=\"60 FPM (1Hz, 50ms ON / 950ms OFF)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Strobe pattern\" v=\"Single flash per period (ICAO daisy-chain\"/>\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <Note c={C.green} ch=\"WS2812C-2020 has a 2\u00d72mm footprint \u2014 small enough to recess into printed nacelle tip caps. The port and starboard LEDs are encapsulated in a clear PETG lens diffuser that's flush with the nacelle leading edge.\"/>\n          <Note c={C.yellow} ch=\"14 CFR 91.209 requires anti-collision lights for ALL operations. FAA AC 107-2B (Part 107 night ops) requires anti-collision lights visible for 3 statute miles. WS2812C at 60mA white draws ~1.2W \u2014 negligible on the power budget.\"/>\n          <Note c={C.orange} ch=\"The Serenity's nacelle tips point aft in cruise \u2014 the port and starboard LED coverage arcs rotate with the nacelle. In cruise (0\u00b0) coverage is forward-left and forward-right as required. In hover (90\u00b0) coverage is upward-left and upward-right \u2014 acceptable since the anti-collision dorsal and belly strobes provide 360\u00b0 coverage in the critical horizontal plane.\"/>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"Pico 2 Firmware \u2014 LED Schedule\"/>\n      {[\n        [\"POWER ON (pre-arm)\",\"Tail white steady ON \u00b7 belly strobe 60 FPM \u00b7 port/stbd OFF\"],\n        [\"ARMED (motors live)\",\"Port red steady \u00b7 stbd green steady \u00b7 tail white \u00b7 dorsal anti-col 60 FPM \u00b7 belly strobe 60 FPM\"],\n        [\"LANDING / HOVER OPS\",\"Landing light ON (nose, full white) \u00b7 all nav lights maintained\"],\n        [\"TRANSITION\",\"Port/stbd LEDs pulse amber briefly during nacelle sweep (status indicator)\"],\n        [\"LOW BATTERY strobe continues per regulation\"],\n      ].map(([state,desc],i)=>(\n        <div key={i} style={{display:\"flex\",gap:12,padding:\"7px 0\",\n          borderBottom:\"1px solid ANTENNA TAB \u2014 Serenity body adapted\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\nfunction AntennaDiagram(){\n  const VW=700, VH=320, CY=160, SC=(mm)=>mm*1.28, NX=55, xp=(mm)=>NX+SC(mm);\n  const PROF=[[0,0],[8,10],[22,18],[40,30],[58,36],[88,37],[120,42],[140,42],[165,40],[190,36],[220,29],[252,18],[260,16],[278,24],[305,29],[330,28],[360,22]];\n  const upper=PROF.map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY-SC(y)]);\n  const lower=[...PROF].reverse().map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY+SC(y)]);\n  const outline=[...upper,...lower].map((p,i)=>i===0?`M${p[0]},${p[1]}`:`L${p[0]},${p[1]}`).join(\" \")+\"Z\";\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" spar\"/>\n          <Note c={C.purple} ch=\"Both W5500 chips are on separate SPI buses (TRIHAT: SPI1; COMPHAT: SPI1 on CM4 GPIO 17-21). The JST-GH Ethernet cable connects the two W5500 magnetics outputs directly \u2014 equivalent to a short Cat5e patch lead. Auto-MDIX is handled in hardware by both W5500s; no crossover required.\"/>\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <SH t=\"CAN FD \u2014 TRIHAT-1 J2 \u2194 COMPHAT-1 J1\" mt={0} c={C.orange}/>\n          <KV k=\"Connector\" v=\"4-pin JST-GH 1.25mm pitch (both ends)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Cable\" v=\"Twisted pair (CANH/CANL) + 2\u00d7 power \u00b7 120mm\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 1\" v=\"GND\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 2\" v=\"+5V (bus power from TRIHAT BEC rail)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 3\" v=\"CANH\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 4\" v=\"CANL\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Standard\" v=\"ISO 11898-1:2015 CAN FD\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Data rate\" v=\"1Mbps nominal / 4Mbps FD data phase\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Transceivers\" v=\"MCP2562FD on both boards \u2014 3.3V logic / 5V bus\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Termination\" v=\"120\u03a9 at TRIHAT-1 J2 (endpoint) \u00b7 none at COMPHAT-1 (midpoint if extended)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Protocol\" v=\"DroneCAN / UAVCANv1 framing \u00b7 custom Pico\u2194CM4 messages\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pico\u2192CM4 msgs\" v=\"AHRS state \u00b7 ESC RPM \u00b7 sensor health \u00b7 arm/disarm events\"/>\n          <KV k=\"CM4\u2192Pico msgs\" v=\"Mission waypoints \u00b7 mode commands \u00b7 parameter updates\"/>\n          <KV k=\"CANH/CANL twist\" v=\"\u226525 twists/metre \u00b7 min 20mm bend radius \u00b7 away from power wires\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Route cable\" v=\"Starboard keel spine \u00b7 separated \u226520mm from Ethernet cable\"/>\n          <Note c={C.orange} ch=\"CANH/CANL must be twisted pair \u2014 run JST-GH cable with the signal pair twisted before insertion into the connector. If using raw wire, pre-twist at 1 twist per 20mm minimum. Do NOT route CAN cable parallel to ESC power wires for more than 30mm \u2014 use physical separation or 90\u00b0 crossings.\"/>\n          <Warn ch=\"Both boards are CAN bus endpoints in this 2-node topology. The 120\u03a9 termination resistor solder bridge on TRIHAT-1 MUST be populated. COMPHAT-1 termination bridge must NOT be populated (it is a single internal bus segment; stub impedance would be wrong). Verify with a CAN analyser before first arming.\"/>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n      <SH t=\"Cable Routing in Serenity Hull\"/>\n      {[\n        [\"Ethernet (purple)\",\"Port keel spine \u00b7 enters TRIHAT-1 through bulkhead at 90mm \u00b7 exits avionics bay at 160mm \u00b7 enters COMPHAT-1 \u00b7 total run ~150mm \u00b7 6-pin JST-GH\"],\n        [\"CAN FD (orange)\",\"Starboard keel spine to main bus at 135mm \u00b7 branches to each ESC via XT30\"],\n        [\"Signal (servo/ESC PWM)\",\"Bundled with ESC signal wires \u00b7 28AWG silicone \u00b7 from TRIHAT-1 GP14-18\"],\n        [\"LED chain\",\"28AWG 3-wire (5V/GND/DATA) \u00b7 port nacelle \u2192 stbd nacelle \u2192 tail \u2192 dorsal \u2192 belly \u00b7 total ~900mm\"],\n        [\"Pitot tubing\",\"2\u00d7 2mm ID silicone battery)\",v:`${AUW_BASE}g`,c:C.dim,s:\"Fixed mass\"},\n          {l:\"Max battery mass\",v:`${Math.floor(THRUST_NAC/2-AUW_BASE)}g`,c:C.orange,s:\"For T/W = 2.0 exactly\"},\n          {l:\"Nacelle ${s.c}44`,background:`${s.c}07`,borderRadius:4}}>\n          <div style={{color:C.dim,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,letterSpacing:\"0.08em\",marginBottom:3}}>{s.l}</div>\n          <div style={{color:s.c,fontFamily:M,fontSize:18,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>{s.v}</div>\n          <div style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,marginTop:2}}>{s.s}</div>\n        </div>))}\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"Battery Candidates\"/>\n      <div style={{overflowX:\"auto\"}}>\n        <table style={{width:\"100%\",borderCollapse:\"collapse\",fontFamily:M,fontSize:11}}>\n          <TH cols={[\"ID\",\"BATTERY\",\"BRAND\",\"MASS\",\"CAP\",\"C-CONT\",\"MAX A\",\"AUW\",\"T/W\",\"HOVER\",\"CRUISE\",\"STATUS\"]}/>\n          <tbody>{BATTERIES.map((b,i)=>(\n            <tr key={i} style={{background:b===best?\"rgba(74,222,128,0.07)\":i%2===0?\"rgba(0,229,255,0.025)\":\"transparent\",\n              border:b===best?`1px solid rgba(74,222,128,0.3)`:\"none\"}}>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.yellow,fontWeight:\"bold\",fontFamily:M}}>{b.id}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.text,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{b.name}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.dim,fontSize:10}}>{b.brand}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.orange}}>{b.mass}g</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:b.twOk?C.teal:C.dimmer}}>{b.twOk?b.crsMin+\" min\":\"\u2014\"}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\"}}>\n                {!b.twOk?<span style={{color:C.red,fontSize:10}}>T/W {\"<\"} 2.0 \u2717</span>\n                 :b===best?<span style={{color:C.green,fontWeight:\"bold\",fontSize:10}}>\u2605 RECOMMENDED</span>\n                 :<span style={{color:C.dim,fontSize:10}}>OK</span>}\n              </td>\n            </tr>\n          ))}</tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      {best && (<>\n        <SH t={`Recommended: ${best.name}`} c={C.green}/>\n        <div style={{display:\"grid\",gridTemplateColumns:\"1fr 1fr\",gap:24}}>\n          <div>\n            <KV k=\"Capacity\"         v={`${best.cap} mAh`}/>\n            <KV k=\"Cell count\"       v=\"5S (18.5V nominal)\"/>\n            <KV k=\"C-rating\"         v={`${best.c}C continuous`}/>\n            <KV k=\"Max continuous\"   v={`${best.maxDraw}A`} vc={C.green}/>\n            <KV k=\"Mass\"             v={`${best.mass}g`}/>\n            <KV k=\"AUW with battery\" v={`${best.auw}g`}/>\n            <KV k=\"T/W ratio\"        v={`${best.tw}:1`} vc={C.green}/>\n            <KV k=\"Hover endurance\"  v={`${best.hovrMin} min`} vc={C.yellow}/>\n            <KV k=\"Cruise endurance\" v={`${best.crsMin} min`} vc={C.teal}/>\n            <KV k=\"Connector\"        v=\"XT60 (crimp 12AWG silicone to power bus)\"/>\n            <KV k=\"Dimensions (typ)\" v=\"114 \u00d7 35 \u00d7 29 mm\"/>\n          </div>\n          <div>\n            <Note c={C.green} ch={`${best.name} delivers the best endurance of any battery that keeps T/W \u2265 2.0. At ${best.tw}:1 T/W there is comfortable margin for wind gusts, transition manoeuvres, and up to ~120g of payload without dropping below 2.0.`}/>\n            <Warn ch={`30C continuous batteries require careful in-flight monitoring. At peak draw (~70A all fans WOT) a 30C/${best.cap}mAh pack delivers ${best.maxDraw}A \u2014 just at the limit. Use 45C or higher for aggressive manoeuvres. Tattu Funfly or Gens Ace G-Tech in ${best.cap}mAh 45C are preferred.`}/>\n            <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"Battery slide rail range: \u00b122mm on 2\u00d7 M3 aluminium keel rails. With the heavier battery at this mass, the centroid sits at ~197mm from nose \u2014 within the \u00b122mm trim window. No ballast required.\"/>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </>)}\n\n      <SH t=\"Endurance vs Battery Mass (T/W \u2265 2.0 window)\"/>\n      {/* Inline bar chart */}\n      <div style={{display:\"flex\",flexDirection:\"column\",gap:8,marginTop:8}}>\n        {BATTERIES.filter(b=>b.twOk).map(b=>{\n          const maxH=Math.max(...BATTERIES.filter(x=>x.twOk).map(x=>parseFloat(x.hovrMin)));\n          return(\n            <div key={b.id} style={{display:\"flex\",alignItems:\"center\",gap:10}}>\n              <span style={{color:C.yellow,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:22}}>{b.id}</span>\n              <span style={{color:C.dim,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:165,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{b.name}</span>\n              <div style={{flex:1,background:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.05)\",borderRadius:2,height:14,position:\"relative\"}}>\n                <div style={{width:`${parseFloat(b.hovrMin)/maxH*100}%`,height:\"100%\",\n                  background:b===best?C.green:C.teal,opacity:.7,borderRadius:2}}/>\n              </div>\n              <span style={{color:b===best?C.green:C.teal,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:55}}>{b.hovrMin} min</span>\n              <span style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,minWidth:50}}>T/W {b.tw}</span>\n            </div>\n          );\n        })}\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n// NAVIGATION LIGHTS TAB\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\nfunction NavLightDiagram(){\n  const VW=720, VH=360, CY=180;\n  const SC=(mm)=>mm*1.28, NX=55, xp=(mm)=>NX+SC(mm);\n\n  // hull outline top view\n  const PROF=[[0,0],[8,10],[22,18],[40,30],[58,36],[88,37],[120,42],[140,42],[165,40],[190,36],[220,29],[252,18],[260,16],[278,24],[305,29],[330,28],[360,22]];\n  const upper=PROF.map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY-SC(y)]);\n  const lower=[...PROF].reverse().map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY+SC(y)]);\n  const outline=[...upper,...lower].map((p,i)=>i===0?`M${p[0]},${p[1]}`:`L${p[0]},${p[1]}`).join(\" \")+\"Z\";\n\n  // arc helpers for coverage arcs\n  function arc(cx,cy,r,startDeg,endDeg,color,dash=\"\"){\n    const s=startDeg*Math.PI/180, e=endDeg*Math.PI/180;\n    const x1=cx+r*Math.cos(s), y1=cy+r*Math.sin(s);\n    const x2=cx+r*Math.cos(e), y2=cy+r*Math.sin(e);\n    const large=(endDeg-startDeg)>180?1:0;\n    return <path d={`M${cx},${cy} L${x1},${y1} A${r},${r} 0 ${large},1 ${x2},${y2} Z`}\n      fill={`${color}18`} stroke={color} strokeWidth={0.8} strokeDasharray={dash||\"\"} opacity={0.8}/>;\n  }\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n      {/* Coverage arcs \u2014 top view */}\n      {/* Port red: 110\u00b0 arc centred on left wing tip, facing forward-left */}\n      {arc(xp(155), CY-SC(340), SC(55), -145, -35, \"#ff2020\")}\n      {/* Stbd green: 110\u00b0 arc centred on right wing tip, facing forward-right */}\n      {arc(xp(155), CY+SC(340), SC(55), 35, 145, \"#00dd00\")}\n      {/* Tail white: 140\u00b0 arc, aft */}\n      {arc(xp(350), CY, SC(55), -70, 70, \"#ffffff\")}\n      {/* Anti-collision 360\u00b0 */}\n      <circle cx={xp(120)} cy={CY} r={SC(40)}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,68,68,0.05)\" stroke=\"#ff4444\" strokeWidth={0.8} strokeDasharray=\"4 3\" opacity={0.7}/>\n      {/* Belly strobe 360\u00b0 */}\n      <circle cx={xp(160)} cy={CY} r={SC(35)}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.03)\" stroke=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.3)\" strokeWidth={0.7} strokeDasharray=\"3 3\"/>\n\n      {/* Hull */}\n      <path d={outline} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.06)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      {/* Arms */}\n      {[-1,1].map(side=>(\n        <g key={side}>\n          <line x1={xp(128)} y1={CY+side*SC(50)} x2={xp(160)} y2={CY+side*SC(340)}\n            stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n          {/* Nacelle */}\n          <ellipse cx={xp(155)} cy={CY+side*SC(340)} rx={SC(34)} ry={SC(14)}\n            fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.05)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.2}/>\n        </g>\n      ))}\n\n      {/* Port LED */}\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY-SC(340)} r={7} fill=\"#ff2020\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY-SC(340)} r={14} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#ff2020\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(140)-18} y={CY-SC(340)-16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ff2020\" fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">PORT \u25cf</text>\n\n      {/* Stbd LED */}\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY+SC(340)} r={7} fill=\"#00dd00\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY+SC(340)} r={14} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#00dd00\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(140)+18} y={CY+SC(340)+20} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#00dd00\" fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">STBD \u25cf</text>\n\n      {/* Tail white */}\n      <circle cx={xp(350)} cy={CY} r={7} fill=\"#ffffff\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(350)} cy={CY} r={14} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#ffffff\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(350)+18} y={CY-4} fill=\"#dddddd\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>TAIL \u25cf</text>\n\n      {/* Anti-collision dorsal */}\n      <circle cx={xp(120)} cy={CY} r={7} fill=\"#ff4444\" opacity={0.9}/>\n      <text x={xp(120)} y={CY-14} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ff4444\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">ACOL\u25c6</text>\n\n      {/* Belly strobe */}\n      <circle cx={xp(160)} cy={CY} r={5} fill=\"rgba(255,255,200,0.8)\" stroke=\"#ffffaa\" strokeWidth={0.8}/>\n      <text x={xp(160)} y={CY+16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ffffaa\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>BELLY\u25c6</text>\n\n      {/* Landing light */}\n      <rect x={xp(22)} y={CY-SC(8)} width={SC(16)} height={SC(16)} rx={2}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,255,120,0.2)\" stroke=\"#ffff80\" strokeWidth={1}/>\n      <text x={xp(30)} y={CY-SC(13)} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ffff80\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>LAND</text>\n\n      {/* Nose / tail labels */}\n      <text x={NX-8} y={CY+4} textAnchor=\"end\" fill={C.dimmer} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>NOSE</text>\n      <text x={xp(360)+6} y={CY+4} fill={C.dimmer} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>TAIL</text>\n      <text x={VW/2} y={16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.25)\"\n        fontSize={9} fontFamily={M} letterSpacing=\"0.12em\">NAVIGATION LIGHT LAYOUT \u2014 TOP VIEW \u2014 COVERAGE ARCS SHOWN</text>\n\n      {/* Legend */}\n      {[[\"#ff2020\",\"Port red (steady)\"],[\"#00dd00\",\"Stbd green (steady)\"],\n        [\"#ffffff\",\"Tail white (steady)\"],[\"#ff4444\",\"Anti-col dorsal (strobe)\"],\n        [\"#ffffaa\",\"Belly strobe\"],[\"#ffff80\",\"Landing/hover light\"]].map(([col,lbl],i)=>(\n        <g key={i}>\n          <circle cx={12} cy={240+i*18} r={5} fill={col} opacity={0.9}/>\n          <text x={22} y={244+i*18} fill={col} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M}>{lbl}</text>\n        </g>\n      ))}\n    </svg>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction NavLightsTab(){\n  return(\n    <div>\n      <div style={{background:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.03)\",border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,\n        padding:\"10px 14px\",marginBottom:16,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n        Navigation lights comply with <span style={{color:C.accent}}>ICAO Annex 2</span>,&nbsp;\n        <span style={{color:C.orange}}>14 CFR Part 91.209</span>, and&nbsp;\n        <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>FAA AC 107-2B</span> (sUAS night operations).\n        All LEDs are WS2812C-2020 individually addressable RGB, driven by Pico 2 PIO state machine on GP26.\n      </div>\n\n      <div style={{border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,background:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.01)\",padding:8,marginBottom:20}}>\n        <NavLightDiagram/>\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"Light Specifications\"/>\n      <div style={{overflowX:\"auto\"}}>\n        <table style={{width:\"100%\",borderCollapse:\"collapse\",fontFamily:M,fontSize:10}}>\n          <TH cols={[\"LIGHT\",\"COLOR\",\"POSITION\",\"ARC COVERAGE\",\"FLASH\",\"LUMENS\",\"REGULATION\"]}/>\n          <tbody>{NAV_LIGHTS.map((l,i)=>(\n            <tr key={i} style={{background:i%2===0?\"rgba(0,229,255,0.025)\":\"transparent\",verticalAlign:\"top\"}}>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:l.color,fontWeight:\"bold\",whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{l.label}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\"}}>\n                <span style={{background:l.color,color:\"#000\",padding:\"1px 6px\",borderRadius:2,fontSize:9}}>{l.colorName}</span>\n              </td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.text,fontSize:10}}>{l.pos}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.dim,fontSize:10}}>{l.regs.split(\"\u00b7\")[1]||\"360\u00b0\"}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:l.flash.includes(\"FPM\")?C.orange:C.green,fontSize:10,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{l.flash}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.yellow,fontSize:10,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{l.lumen}</td>\n              <td style={{padding:\"6px 10px\",color:C.dimmer,fontSize:9}}>{l.regs.split(\"\u00b7\")[0]}</td>\n            </tr>\n          ))}</tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"LED Controller Spec\"/>\n      <div style={{display:\"grid\",gridTemplateColumns:\"1fr 1fr\",gap:24}}>\n        <div>\n          <KV k=\"LED type\" v=\"WS2812C-2020 (2020 package, 5V)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Chain length\" v=\"6 LEDs on a single data line\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Data pin\" v=\"Pico 2 GP26 \u2192 PIO0 state machine\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Protocol\" v=\"800kHz NZR (WS2812 standard)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Power\" v=\"5V from BEC \u00b7 max 60mA per LED at white\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Total LED current\" v=\"~180mA max (all white full brightness)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Anti-collision rate\" v=\"60 FPM (1Hz, 50ms ON / 950ms OFF)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Strobe pattern\" v=\"Single flash per period (ICAO compliant)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Arm interlock\" v=\"Navigation lights activate on arm, strobe on power-on\"/>\n          <KV k=\"LED wire\" v=\"28AWG silicone 3-wire (5V, GND, DATA) daisy-chain\"/>\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <Note c={C.green} ch=\"WS2812C-2020 has a 2\u00d72mm footprint \u2014 small enough to recess into printed nacelle tip caps. The port and starboard LEDs are encapsulated in a clear PETG lens diffuser that's flush with the nacelle leading edge.\"/>\n          <Note c={C.yellow} ch=\"14 CFR 91.209 requires anti-collision lights for ALL operations. FAA AC 107-2B (Part 107 night ops) requires anti-collision lights visible for 3 statute miles. WS2812C at 60mA white draws ~1.2W \u2014 negligible on the power budget.\"/>\n          <Note c={C.orange} ch=\"The Serenity's nacelle tips point aft in cruise \u2014 the port and starboard LED coverage arcs rotate with the nacelle. In cruise (0\u00b0) coverage is forward-left and forward-right as required. In hover (90\u00b0) coverage is upward-left and upward-right \u2014 acceptable since the anti-collision dorsal and belly strobes provide 360\u00b0 coverage in the critical horizontal plane.\"/>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <SH t=\"Pico 2 Firmware \u2014 LED Schedule\"/>\n      {[\n        [\"POWER ON (pre-arm)\",\"Tail white steady ON \u00b7 belly strobe 60 FPM \u00b7 port/stbd OFF\"],\n        [\"ARMED (motors live)\",\"Port red steady \u00b7 stbd green steady \u00b7 tail white \u00b7 dorsal anti-col 60 FPM \u00b7 belly strobe 60 FPM\"],\n        [\"LANDING / HOVER OPS\",\"Landing light ON (nose, full white) \u00b7 all nav lights maintained\"],\n        [\"TRANSITION\",\"Port/stbd LEDs pulse amber briefly during nacelle sweep (status indicator)\"],\n        [\"LOW BATTERY \u226420%\",\"Port and stbd LEDs flash alternating red/white at 2Hz\"],\n        [\"FAILSAFE / RTL\",\"All lights flash simultaneously at 2Hz\"],\n        [\"DISARMED\",\"Tail white steady \u00b7 strobe continues per regulation\"],\n      ].map(([state,desc],i)=>(\n        <div key={i} style={{display:\"flex\",gap:12,padding:\"7px 0\",\n          borderBottom:\"1px solid rgba(0,229,255,0.07)\"}}>\n          <span style={{color:C.teal,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,minWidth:180,flexShrink:0}}>{state}</span>\n          <span style={{color:C.dim,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,lineHeight:1.6}}>{desc}</span>\n        </div>\n      ))}\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n// ANTENNA TAB \u2014 Serenity body adapted\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\nfunction AntennaDiagram(){\n  const VW=700, VH=320, CY=160, SC=(mm)=>mm*1.28, NX=55, xp=(mm)=>NX+SC(mm);\n  const PROF=[[0,0],[8,10],[22,18],[40,30],[58,36],[88,37],[120,42],[140,42],[165,40],[190,36],[220,29],[252,18],[260,16],[278,24],[305,29],[330,28],[360,22]];\n  const upper=PROF.map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY-SC(y)]);\n  const lower=[...PROF].reverse().map(([x,y])=>[xp(x),CY+SC(y)]);\n  const outline=[...upper,...lower].map((p,i)=>i===0?`M${p[0]},${p[1]}`:`L${p[0]},${p[1]}`).join(\" \")+\"Z\";\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n      <path d={outline} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.05)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      {/* Wing arms */}\n      {[-1,1].map(side=>(\n         <KV k=\"Speed\" v=\"100BASE-T \u00b7 100Mbps full duplex\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Protocol\" v=\"UDP (MAVLink relay) \u00b7 TCP (config/OTA) \u00b7 ICMP\"/>\n          <KV k=\"IP addressing\" The JST-GH Ethernet cable connects the two W5500 magnetics outputs directly \u2014 equivalent to a short Cat5e patch lead. Auto-MDIX is handled in hardware by both W5500s; no crossover required.\"/>\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <SH t=\"CAN FD \u2014 TRIHAT-1 J2 \u2194 COMPHAT-1 J1\" mt={0} c={C.orange}/>\n          <KV k=\"Connector\" v=\"4-pin JST-GH 1.25mm pitch (both ends)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Cable\" v=\"Twisted pair (CANH/CANL) + 2\u00d7 power \u00b7 120mm\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 1\" v=\"GND\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 2\" v=\"+5V (bus power from TRIHAT BEC rail)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 3\" v=\"CANH\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pin 4\" v=\"CANL\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Standard\" v=\"ISO 11898-1:2015 CAN FD\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Data rate\" v=\"1Mbps nominal / 4Mbps FD data phase\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Transceivers\" v=\"MCP2562FD on both boards \u2014 3.3V logic / 5V bus\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Termination\" v=\"120\u03a9 at TRIHAT-1 J2 (endpoint) \u00b7 none at COMPHAT-1 (midpoint if extended)\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Protocol\" v=\"DroneCAN / UAVCANv1 framing \u00b7 custom Pico\u2194CM4 messages\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Pico\u2192CM4 msgs\" v=\"AHRS state \u00b7 ESC RPM \u00b7 sensor health \u00b7 arm/disarm events\"/>\n          <KV k=\"CM4\u2192Pico msgs\" v=\"Mission waypoints \u00b7 mode commands \u00b7 parameter updates\"/>\n          <KV k=\"CANH/CANL twist\" v=\"\u226525 twists/metre \u00b7 min 20mm bend radius \u00b7 away from power wires\"/>\n          <KV k=\"Route cable\" v=\"Starboard keel spine \u00b7 separated \u226520mm from Ethernet cable\"/>\n          <Note c={C.orange} ch=\"CANH/CANL must be twisted pair \u2014 run JST-GH cable with the signal pair twisted before insertion into the connector. COMPHAT-1 termination bridge must NOT be populated (it is a single internal bus segment; stub impedance would be wrong). \")+\"Z\";\n}\nfunction Arms({CY,stroke=C.accent}){\n  return[-1,1].map(side=>(\n    <g key={side}>\n      <line x1={xp(128)} y1={CY+side*SC(50)} x2={xp(160)} y2={CY+side*SC(340)} stroke={stroke} 1 \u00b7 OVERVIEW\n// NavDiagram(){\n  const VW=720,VH=360,CY=180;\n  const arc=(cx,cy,r,a1,a2,col)=>{\n    const s=a1*Math.PI/180,e=a2*Math.PI/180;\n    const x1=cx+r*Math.cos(s),y1=cy+r*Math.sin(s),x2=cx+r*Math.cos(e),y2=cy+r*Math.sin(e);\n    return<path d={`M${cx},${cy} L${x1},${y1} A${r},${r} 0 ${a2-a1>180?1:0},1 ${x2},${y2} Z`}\n      fill={`${col}18`} stroke={col} strokeWidth={0.8} opacity={0.8}/>;\n  };\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n      {/* coverage arcs */}\n      {arc(xp(140),CY-SC(340),SC(52),-145,-35,\"#ff2020\")}\n      {arc(xp(140),CY+SC(340),SC(52),35,145,\"#00cc00\")}\n      {arc(xp(350),CY,SC(52),-70,70,\"#ffffff\")}\n      <circle cx={xp(120)} cy={CY} r={SC(38)} fill=\"rgba(255,50,50,0.04)\" stroke=\"#ff4444\" strokeWidth={0.8} strokeDasharray=\"4 3\" opacity={0.7}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(160)} cy={CY} r={SC(33)} fill=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.02)\" r={7} fill=\"#ff2020\" opacity={0.95}/>\n      <circle cx={xp(140)} cy={CY-SC(340)} r={16} fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#ff2020\" strokeWidth={0.7} opacity={0.4}/>\n      <text x={xp(140)-22} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#00cc00\" fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">STBD \u25cf</text>\n      {/* tail */}\n      <circle cx={xp(350)} cy={CY} r={7} fill=\"#eeeeee\" opacity={0.95}/>\n      <text x={xp(350)+20} y={CY-2} fill=\"#cccccc\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>TAIL \u25cf</text>\n      {/* anti-collision dorsal */}\n      <circle cx={xp(120)} cy={CY} r={7} fill=\"#ff4444\" opacity={0.95}/>\n      <text x={xp(120)} y={CY-16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"#ff4444\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">ACOL\u25c6</text>\n      {/* belly strobe fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>BELLY\u25c6</text>\n      {/* landing */}\n      <rect x={xp(22)} y={CY-SC(8)} width={SC(16)} height={SC(16)} rx={2} fill=\"rgba(255,255,120,0.2)\" stroke=\"#ffff80\" fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>TAIL</text>\n    </svg>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction NavLightsTab(){\n  const lights=[\n    {id:\"PORT\", col:\"#ff2020\",name:\"Red\",   pos:\"Left nacelle leading edge tip\", NavDiagram(){\n  const VW=720,VH=360,CY=180;\n  const arc=(cx,cy,r,a1,a2,col)=>{\n    const s=a1*Math.PI/180,e=a2*Math.PI/180;\n    const That makes the **5S 2800mAh 45C** (271g, T/W **2.04:1**) the sweet spot \u2014 it's the largest pack that comfortably stays above the limit. The 5S 3000mAh 45C at 292g drops T/W to 1.99, just outside. At 2800mAh you get **6.4 min hover / 7.9 min cruise** on the recommended Tattu Funfly or GNB variant. The 45C rating delivers 126A peak capability against a ~70A worst-case draw \u2014 1.8\u00d7 margin.\n\n**Navigation Lights (Tab 3):** Six WS2812C-2020 LEDs on a single PIO data line (GP26). Port red and starboard green ride in recessed clear PETG tip caps in the nacelle leading edges \u2014 they rotate with the nacelles, which is acceptable because the dorsal anti-collision strobe and belly strobe maintain 360\u00b0 horizontal-plane coverage in all flight modes.         "title": "EDF Thrust Tube - Radio Control Info",
        "url": "https://www.radiocontrolinfo.com/rc-planes/edf-jets/edf-thrust-tube/",
        "metadata": {
          "type": "webpage_metadata",
          "site_domain": "radiocontrolinfo.com",
          "favicon_url": "https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?sz=64&domain=radiocontrolinfo.com",

        "is_missing": false,
        "text": "It is a balance between thrust and speed that must be achieved according to your goals. One who desires a faster top speed may require a smaller exhaust diameter. One who desires more vertical performance requires a larger exit diameter.\n\nIn an electric ducted fan jet, the air flow creates the thrust for forward movement of the plane. This airflow must exit through ducting work in the airplane known as the EDF Thrust Tube. Once the air reaches the fan unit, the motor accelerates the air and pushes it out the rear of the fan. An EDF thrust tube is placed at the rear of the fan and extends to the very end at the exhaust side of the jet. A thrust tube can provide many benefits but does share trade offs. A thrust tube is used to control the surface area of the air escaping out the back of the ducted fan jet. By changing the diameter of the exit on a ducted fan jet, a couple parameters are changed. By decreasing the exhaust exit diameter the airspeed rushing out will increase. This may also increase the maximum speed of your model. By increasing the exhaust exit diameter, additional thrust may be gained.\n\nIt is a balance between thrust and speed that must be achieved according to your goals. One who desires a faster top speed may require a smaller exhaust diameter. One who desires more vertical performance requires a larger exit diameter. It is important to understand that these to variables are trade offs. Increasing exhaust exit speeds will decrease thrust and vice versa. The exhaust exit diameter is best achieved by use of a calculation. Below you will find the link to the online calculator that will simplify this step. First we will cover the details and terminology. There are two critical dimensions needed in order to perform this calculation. The first dimension is the inner case diameter. This is the diameter of the case on the inside where the fan blades pass by. The second critical dimension is the outer hub diameter. On an EDF unit, the outer hub is the cylinder that houses the brushless motor.",

It has a rotating inner ring that opens/closes flap segments. The original was designed for a specific EDF size, but the Fusion 360 file was uploaded for remixing.\n\nThe variable-area nozzle concept:\n- Inner ring rotates via servo to open/close nozzle flaps\n- Flaps pivot around their attachment points\n- As the ring rotates, it changes the exit area of the nozzle\n- More area = more thrust (at the cost of speed)\n- Less area = more speed (at cost of thrust)\n\nFor the Serenity drone:\n- The engine bell in hover position needs MAXIMUM thrust area (high disk loading)\n- In cruise, we want a smaller exit area to accelerate airflow for speed\n- A variable nozzle on the 40mm EDF in the Serenity bell could provide this\n\nFor our remix:\n- Scale to fit 40mm EDF (inner duct = 40mm)\n- Integrate with Serenity engine bell aesthetics\n- Add a small SG90/SG92 servo to actuate the variable area ring\n- The Pico 2 controls it via an additional PWM channel\n- Area ratio range: 85% to 110% of duct area (approximately)\n\nNow let me plan the EDF upgrade:\n\n**70mm EDFs (recommended - I'll aim for a clean, well-organized file.\n\nFor the variable nozzle remix section:\n- BamJr thing:2991269 is a variable-area nozzle with rotating inner ring\n- For our remix: scale inner ring to 40mm ID (for 40mm EDF)\n- Outer housing integrates with Serenity bell aesthetic\n- The flaps vary the exit area from ~85% to ~115% of duct area\n- Servo: SG90, actuating a crank arm on the rotating ring\n- Pico 2 GP15 \u2192 PWM \u2192 SG90\n\nVariable nozzle theory for the 40mm EDF:\n- EDF duct area: \u03c0 \u00d7 (40/2)\u00b2 = \u03c0 \u00d7 400 = 1257 mm\u00b2\n- Optimal exit for max thrust: ~105% of duct area (slight expansion) \u2192 exit diameter \u2248 41mm\n- Optimal exit for max speed: ~80% of duct area (convergent) \u2192 exit diameter \u2248 36mm\n- Variable range: 36mm to 41mm exit diameter, controlled by servo\n- In hover trim: open (41mm, more thrust)\n- In cruise: closed (36mm, more jet speed, better propulsive efficiency at higher airspeed)\n\nThis is actually quite useful since in hover the fwd EDF doesn't contribute much to vertical thrust (it's horizontal), and in cruise you want jet velocity to be well above flight speed. Variable nozzle makes the 40mm fwd EDF useful across both regimes.\n\nLet me write the comprehensive file now.",

      <KV k=\"Remix changes\" v=\"Scale inner ring to 40mm ID \u00b7 outer housing to Serenity bell profile\"/>\n        <KV k=\"Flap count\" v=\"8 (matches original) \u00b7 10mm chord \u00b7 4mm closed; adjust pushrod length\"/>\n        <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"The variable nozzle is not required for flight \u2014 a fixed nozzle at 100% area works fine. The variable nozzle adds ~15% more useful thrust range and meaningfully improves cruise Isp at speeds above 18 m/s. Consider building fixed first, then adding variable nozzle after initial flight testing.\"/>\n        <Note c={C.yellow} ch=\"Attribution required: 'Variable-area nozzle based on BamJr / Thingiverse thing:2991269, CC BY 4.0. style={{color:LAYER_COL[s.layer]||C.dim,border:`1px solid ${LAYER_COL[s.layer]||C.dim}40`,padding:\"1px 5px\",borderRadius:2,fontSize:8,whiteSpace:\"nowrap\"}}>{s.layer}</span></td>\n          <td </tr>))}</tbody>\n      </table>\n    </div>\n    <Note c={C.accent} ch=\"Variable nozzle controller added as new Pico 2 algorithm module. GPL-3 (QGC, SiK) must remain open-source if redistributed. Proprietary Pico 2 firmware uses BSD/MIT only. BamJr nozzle remix: CC BY 4.0 \u2014 attribution required.\"/>\n  </div>);\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n//  TAB 9 \u2014 BUILD GUIDE\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\nconst BUILD_PHASES=[\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 1 \u00b7 PRINT PREP\",c:C.teal,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Source filament\",d:\"PETG (hull, bell, nacelle fairings, skids carrier): min 300g. CF-PETG (nacelle pods, tilt brackets, nozzle inner ring): min 80g. TPU 95A (skid feet): 40g. Dry all filament 6h at 65\u00b0C before printing structural parts.\"},\n      {t:\"Printer prep\",d:\"Install hardened steel nozzle (0.4mm) \u2014 mandatory for CF-PETG. Calibrate E-steps and PA (Pressure Advance) for each filament. Bed: PEI spring steel sheet. Level after nozzle change.\"},\n      {t:\"Slice hull sections\",d:\"Split hull at four planes: nose cap (0\u201322mm), cockpit (22\u201390mm), mid-hull left+right halves (90\u2013230mm split down centreline), aft-hull (230\u2013270mm), engine bell (270\u2013365mm). Print each flat on bed. 8% gyroid infill, 2 perimeters, 0.20mm layer height for all hull sections. Enable seam alignment to dorsal for clean exterior seam.\"},\n      {t:\"Print sequence\",d:\"Print TPU skid feet first (separate job, purge before PETG). Then PETG hull sections (longest jobs, run overnight). Then CF-PETG nacelle pods and tilt brackets last (shorter but critical \u2014 zero tolerance on bearing seats). Finally, variable nozzle inner ring (CF-PETG, print vertically) and flap ring (PETG).\"},\n      {t:\"Post-print\",d:\"Remove supports. Dry-fit all sections before bonding. Nacelle bearing seats: test-fit 8mm OD \u00d7 5mm ID \u00d7 2.5mm bearing with slight interference (use a M5 bolt and nut to press it). Lightly sand all mating surfaces with 220-grit. Clean with IPA.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 2 \u00b7 CF SKELETON\",c:C.accent,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Cut CF keel spine\",d:\"6\u00d73mm flat CF bar \u00b7 385mm length (hull 365mm + 20mm aft overhang for engine bell mount). Mark datum lines at 90mm, 130mm, 160mm, 200mm, 290mm, 365mm from nose. Deburr ends with 180-grit on a block.\"},\n      {t:\"Cut outrigger spars\",d:\"12mm OD CF tube \u00b7 2 pieces \u00d7 300mm. Sand one end of each to 8mm dia \u00d7 25mm to create a reduced tenon that inserts into the hull spar pocket at 130\u2013155mm.\"},\n      {t:\"Drill keel attachment holes\",d:\"3mm pilot holes through keel at 95mm, 165mm, 235mm, 295mm, 360mm for M3 standoffs that hold hull sections. Use drill press for perpendicularity. Countersink to accept M3 flat-head screws.\"},\n      {t:\"Epoxy mid-hull to keel\",d:\"Apply 24h West System 105/206 epoxy to keel at hull attachment points. Slide keel through all hull sections. Clamp and cure 12h. Do NOT bond cockpit cap or aft bell yet \u2014 they need to be removable for electronics access.\"},\n      {t:\"Install spar-to-keel bracket\",d:\"At 130mm, epoxy the 3D-printed CF-PETG spar junction bracket to the keel. The outrigger spars press-fit into this bracket. Check 90\u00b0 alignment from hull centreline with a set square. Tape and cure 12h.\"},\n      {t:\"Install outrigger spars\",d:\"Press-fit spar tenons into bracket. Apply Loctite 638 retaining compound at junction. Check span is symmetric (measure tip-to-tip). Cure 4h before attaching any loads. The spars should flex slightly \u2014 this is correct for crash absorption.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 3 \u00b7 NACELLE ASSEMBLY\",c:C.orange,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Press bearings\",d:\"Press one 8\u00d75\u00d72.5mm ball bearing into each nacelle pod pivot socket using a bearing press jig (3D-print a simple cup jig). The bearing must be flush or \u00b10.2mm. Press the second bearing into the CF-PETG tilt bracket.\"},\n      {t:\"Thread CF pivot rod\",d:\"8mm CF rod \u00b7 52mm long \u00b7 through spar arm \u2192 tilt bracket \u2192 nacelle bearing. Secure with M3 set screw on outer face. Apply a drop of Loctite 243 (medium) to the set screw thread.\"},\n      {t:\"Install nacelle EDF\",d:\"For 70mm EDF: slide into nacelle pod from the top (intake face). The motor mount ears align with M2.5 captive nuts pre-inserted into the pod. Torque all 4\u00d7 M2.5 screws to 0.3 N\u00b7m. Route motor leads through the 4mm wire channel in the nacelle wall and through the hollow CF spar toward the ESC.\"},\n      {t:\"Install tilt servo\",d:\"MG90S servo mounts to CF-PETG tilt bracket via M2 screws and 5mm aluminium standoffs. Connect servo horn to nacelle push-rod anchor at 18mm radius. Servo travel: 90\u00b0 from centrepoint gives full 0\u00b0\u201390\u00b0 nacelle rotation. Check no binding at endpoints.\"},\n      {t:\"LED in nacelle tip\",d:\"Solder short lead to WS2812C-2020 LED (port = red, stbd = green). Epoxy LED into 2\u00d72mm recess in clear PETG tip cap. Apply a tiny dot of 5-minute epoxy over LED for moisture sealing. Slip-fit cap over nacelle tip.\"},\n      {t:\"Final nacelle check\",d:\"Manually rotate nacelle through full 90\u00b0 arc. It should turn freely with no binding, return to any position held without servo. Measure nacelle pod edge-to-spar clearance at both extremes \u2014 minimum 2mm. Check LED wires do not snag on pivot.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 4 \u00b7 ELECTRONICS INSTALLATION\",c:C.purple,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Install Pico 2 + TRIHAT-1\",d:\"Mount Pico 2 on TRIHAT-1 using supplied 2\u00d720 female header. Stack mounts at 60\u201390mm from nose on 4\u00d7 M2.5 nylon standoffs (8mm height) on the cockpit CF bulkhead plate (cut from 2mm CF sheet: 55\u00d738mm). Secure with M2.5 nylon nuts \u2014 use nylon throughout this bay to reduce RF interference.\"},\n      {t:\"Install CM4 stack\",d:\"CM4 Lite plugs into CM4-CARRIER-1 DF40 underside connectors. COMPHAT-1 stacks on top via 2\u00d720 header. This 3-board sandwich mounts at 95\u2013160mm on the avionics bay floor using 4\u00d7 M2.5 aluminium standoffs (11mm). OS microSD installs in CM4-CARRIER-1 slot (bottom of stack). Log microSD in COMPHAT-1 slot (top of stack).\"},\n      {t:\"Install GPS module\",d:\"TRIHAT-1 onboard M10Q is pre-soldered. The U.FL coax runs from TRIHAT-1 to the GPS patch antenna on the cockpit roof (via 3mm access hole in cockpit bulkhead). Secure coax with a small PLA cable guide. Do not kink the U.FL connector \u2014 minimum bend radius 10mm.\"},\n      {t:\"Install airspeed sensor\",d:\"MS4525DO mounts on TRIHAT-1 via the JST-ZH I\u00b2C connector included with the board. Route 2\u00d72mm ID silicone tubing from the sensor pressure ports to the pitot tube port at the nose (stagnation line) and a static port on the hull side at 80mm. Colour-code: red=stagnation, white=static.\"},\n      {t:\"Install ESCs\",d:\"Mount 3 ESCs on the keel underside with double-sided foam tape and one M2.5 screw through their mounting tab. ESC-L and ESC-R for the nacelles at 145\u2013155mm from nose. ESC-fwd for the 40mm EDF at 285\u2013295mm. Route motor leads along the spar/keel channels with cable ties every 30mm.\"},\n      {t:\"Install SiK and RCRS\",d:\"SiK 915MHz air unit sits inside the COMPHAT-1 radio compartment. Its IPEX pigtail exits through the fuselage to the SMA-RP bulkhead connector at the belly 238mm point. 49MHz RCRS module coax routes from COMPHAT-1 to the dorsal antenna fin at 290mm via the starboard keel spine. Secure both pigtails with a P-clip at the keel.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 5 \u00b7 VARIABLE NOZZLE ASSEMBLY\",c:C.yellow,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Print nozzle parts\",d:\"Print inner rotating ring in CF-PETG vertically (layer lines perpendicular to ring axis for best hoop strength). Print 8 flaps in PETG flat. Print outer housing in PETG at 25% infill. Test-fit ring inside housing \u2014 it should rotate with light finger pressure with no wobble.\"},\n      {t:\"Assemble flaps\",d:\"Insert M1.5\u00d78mm pivot pins through flap hinge holes. Seat pivot pins in outer housing recesses. Each flap should pivot freely around its pin. Apply one drop of light machine oil (sewing machine oil) to each pivot pin.\"},\n      {t:\"Install inner ring\",d:\"Screw inner ring into outer housing \u2014 the BamJr design uses a coarse thread to raise/lower the ring, which cams the flaps. Install the taller inner ring variant for greater expansion range. Rotate ring by hand: CCW = flaps open (hover), CW = flaps close (cruise). Verify smooth cam action.\"},\n      {t:\"Mount SG90 servo\",d:\"Secure SG90 to the M2.5 boss on the outer housing using the included servo bracket and M2 screws. Attach servo horn at 12 o'clock (90\u00b0 centre position). Connect 1.5mm pushrod from horn outer hole to the cam arm on the inner ring \u2014 18mm from ring centre. Adjust pushrod length so horn centre = nozzle 100% area.\"},\n      {t:\"Install nozzle in engine bell\",d:\"Insert assembled nozzle from the aft of the engine bell. The outer housing clips into the bell's 70mm ID recess with a quarter-turn bayonet lock (3D-printed tabs). Servo cable routes along the keel to Pico 2 GP15. The aft LED (tail) mounts in the engine bell ring recess above the nozzle.\"},\n      {t:\"Nozzle calibration\",d:\"Connect Pico 2 to computer via USB. In calibration mode, command GP15 to 1.0ms (open) and measure nozzle exit diameter with calipers: target 42mm. Command 2.0ms (closed): target 36mm. If out of spec, adjust pushrod length at the clevis end. Lock with Loctite 243.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 6 \u00b7 WIRING & POWER\",c:C.green,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Power bus installation\",d:\"Mount XT60 power distribution board at 130mm on keel underside. Run 12AWG red/black silicone from battery bay connector to PDB. Run 12AWG silicone from PDB to each ESC. Solder all joints with 60/40 rosin-core solder. Use silicone sleeving over all bare joints \u2014 no heat shrink near printed parts.\"},\n      {t:\"BEC installation\",d:\"5V 3A switching BEC takes input from PDB +5V tap. BEC output (5V/GND) connects to: Pico 2 VSYS \u00b7 CM4 carrier J_PWR \u00b7 servo rail (shared GND, 5V). Use JST-PH 2-pin for BEC output connectors. Measure BEC output with multimeter: must be 5.0V \u00b1 0.1V with all loads connected.\"},\n      {t:\"Ethernet inter-board cable\",d:\"Route 6-pin JST-GH Ethernet cable (150mm) from TRIHAT-1 J3 along the port keel spine to COMPHAT-1 J2. Cable-tie every 40mm. Do not route alongside ESC power cables. Verify no tension on connectors \u2014 leave 20mm service loop at each end.\"},\n      {t:\"CAN FD inter-board cable\",d:\"Route 4-pin JST-GH CAN FD cable (120mm) from TRIHAT-1 J2 along the starboard keel spine to COMPHAT-1 J1. Maintain \u226520mm separation from the Ethernet cable. Verify TRIHAT-1 120\u03a9 termination solder bridge is populated. Verify COMPHAT-1 bridge is NOT populated.\"},\n      {t:\"Signal wiring\",d:\"Connect nacelle servo L/R to TRIHAT-1 GP17/18 (PWM channels). Nozzle servo to TRIHAT-1 GP15. Release servo to TRIHAT-1 GP22. Winch DRV8833 IN1/IN2 to TRIHAT-1 GP22/23. LED chain data to TRIHAT-1 GP26. Label each connector with a Dymo label or colour-coded heat shrink at both ends.\"},\n      {t:\"Continuity check\",d:\"Before applying any power: use multimeter to verify no shorts between +V and GND on: main bus, BEC output, USB5V, 3.3V rails. Verify no continuity between motor phases and ESC power rails (insulation check). Then connect battery via XT60 to a current-limited bench supply at 2A max \u2014 verify no smoke, excessive heat, or unexpected current draw.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 7 \u00b7 SOFTWARE SETUP\",c:C.pink,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Flash Pico 2\",d:\"Download Pico SDK v2.0+. Clone flight controller repo. Build with cmake targeting RP2350. Flash via USB drag-drop (hold BOOTSEL during power-on). Verify heartbeat LED (GP25) blinks at 1Hz after flash.\"},\n      {t:\"Flash CM4 OS\",d:\"Flash Raspberry Pi OS Lite 64-bit (bookworm) to microSD using rpi-imager. In rpi-imager advanced settings: set hostname=serenity, enable SSH, set WiFi SSID/password for home network. Insert SD into CM4-CARRIER-1 slot. Boot CM4 and SSH in.\"},\n      {t:\"Install CM4 software\",d:\"On CM4: apt install mavlink-router python3-pip. pip install mavsdk pymavlink python-can dronecan. Configure mavlink-router to bridge UART0 (SiK) to UDP 14550 and to Pico 2 W5500 at 192.168.10.1:14550. Enable SPI, CAN interfaces via raspi-config.\"},\n      {t:\"Configure TPM\",d:\"apt install tpm2-tools tpm2-tss. Run: tpm2_getcap properties-fixed to verify SLB9670 is detected. Provision device key: tpm2_createprimary + tpm2_create + tpm2_load. Store key handle for runtime attestation. This enables signed telemetry and encrypted OTA updates.\"},\n      {t:\"ESC calibration\",d:\"With no propellers fitted: connect USB to Pico 2. Enter ESC calibration mode. Full throttle on all three ESC channels simultaneously (WOT signal). Connect battery \u2014 ESCs play startup tones. Throttle to zero \u2014 ESCs arm. Run full-throttle 2s test per ESC to verify rotation direction. Reverse any motor phase pair as needed to correct direction.\"},\n      {t:\"Nacelle servo calibration\",d:\"Command left nacelle to 0\u00b0 (cruise) and 90\u00b0 (hover) via Pico 2 calibration routine. Measure with digital angle gauge. Adjust servo arm position or endpoint in firmware to achieve \u00b10.5\u00b0 accuracy at both stops. Verify smooth sweep with no binding. Verify tilt axis is truly perpendicular to airspeed vector.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 8 \u00b7 GROUND TEST\",c:C.lime,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Static balance check\",d:\"Mount on precision balance to find actual CG. Target: 152mm from nose tip. Slide battery rail to achieve target. With 250g payload installed, re-measure: expect CG to shift 5\u20138mm fwd \u2014 slide battery 10mm aft to compensate. Verify T/W at actual AUW.\"},\n      {t:\"Radio link check\",d:\"With SiK ground radio connected to GCS laptop and QGroundControl running: verify MAVLink heartbeat at 1Hz. Verify attitude (roll/pitch/yaw) responds to hand-tilt of aircraft. Verify RC input from 49MHz ground transmitter appears in QGC RC calibration screen. Verify TDDS channel selection \u2014 unit should auto-select clearest channel within 30s of power-on.\"},\n      {t:\"Bench thrust test\",d:\"WITHOUT PROPELLERS/EDF FANS: verify all ESC outputs respond to throttle from GCS or RC. Slowly advance throttle per ESC to verify correct fan rotation direction and no vibration from duct misalignment. THEN: with fans installed, tether aircraft firmly to bench with 4 straps. Advance to 30% throttle per nacelle. Measure bench thrust with luggage scale if available. Verify \u2265180g per 70mm nacelle at 30%.\"},\n      {t:\"Nozzle function test\",d:\"Command nozzle open (hover mode): verify exit dia ~42mm. Command nozzle closed (cruise mode): verify exit dia ~36mm. Verify servo does not bind at either extreme. Verify Pico 2 correctly interpolates nozzle position during a simulated nacelle sweep from 90\u00b0 to 0\u00b0.\"},\n      {t:\"Navigation light test\",d:\"Arm aircraft (motors not running). Verify: port tip = steady red, stbd tip = steady green, tail = steady white, dorsal = flashing red 60FPM, belly = flashing white 60FPM. Disarm: verify port/stbd turn off, tail stays on, strobes continue. Trigger low-battery condition in firmware: verify port+stbd flash alternating red/white 2Hz.\"},\n      {t:\"GPS and sensor check\",d:\"With aircraft outdoors, clear sky view: verify GPS fix within 90s, HDOP \u22641.5, \u22656 satellites in QGC. Spin up EDFs to hover throttle (~46%) \u2014 verify GPS HDOP does not degrade >0.3 with fans running. Verify barometer altitude within \u00b15m of known elevation. Verify airspeed reads 0 \u00b1 0.3 m/s with no pitot flow.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 9 \u00b7 FIRST FLIGHT\",c:C.green,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Pre-flight checks\",d:\"ABCD: Airframe (all hatches closed, propellers secure, no loose wires visible externally), Battery (fully charged, XT60 secure, balance lead plugged into checker showing all cells 4.18\u20134.20V), Communications (RC link active, GCS link active, GPS fix confirmed), Documentation (Part 107 remote pilot cert visible, aircraft registered, location authorised, NOTAMs checked).\"},\n      {t:\"Hover test 1m AGL\",d:\"Fly to 1m AGL in VTOL hover. Hold 30s. Verify stable attitude, no drift. Land. Inspect all screws, servo linkages, ESC temperatures (should be warm but not hot \u2014 under 60\u00b0C with IR thermometer). Verify no unusual vibration sounds from ducts.\"},\n      {t:\"Hover characterisation\",d:\"Fly to 3m AGL. Test roll, pitch, yaw inputs gently (\u00b110\u00b0 excursions). Verify aircraft returns to level when sticks centred. Test altitude hold. Verify landing light activates on command. Record hover amperage from telemetry \u2014 should match calculated hover current within \u00b110%.\"},\n      {t:\"Transition test\",d:\"At \u22658m AGL, command nacelle sweep (hover\u2192cruise transition). Aircraft will accelerate forward. Monitor altitude \u2014 expect \u00b11.5m variation during transition. Verify Pico 2 automatically closes nozzle during sweep. Achieve straight-and-level cruise at \u226515 m/s. Monitor all temperatures and voltages.\"},\n      {t:\"Cruise characterisation\",d:\"In cruise: verify airspeed sensor reads plausibly (\u00b12 m/s of GPS groundspeed in zero wind). Test gentle bank turns \u226420\u00b0. Verify nozzle is in closed position. Record cruise amperage \u2014 should match calculated value. Command transition back to hover and land.\"},\n      {t:\"Cargo mission test\",d:\"Load 250g payload. Swap to cargo battery (2800mAh). Repeat hover test \u2014 confirm aircraft still lifts cleanly (T/W 2.01:1). Test winch deploy: hover at 4m, deploy payload, verify it descends at ~15cm/s. Command winch retract. Land with deployed payload as final test.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n];\n\nfunction BuildGuideTab(){\n  const [open,setOpen]=useState(0);\n  return(<div>\n    <div style={{background:\"rgba(74,222,128,0.05)\",border:`1px solid rgba(74,222,128,0.2)`,borderRadius:4,padding:\"10px 14px\",marginBottom:20,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n      <span style={{color:C.green,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>BUILD SEQUENCE:</span> Follow phases in order. Do not skip ahead \u2014 later phases depend on earlier fits being verified. Estimated build time: <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>40\u201355 hours</span> for an experienced builder. Allow 70+ hours for first-time composite builders.\n    </div>\n    {BUILD_PHASES.map((p,pi)=>(\n      <div key={pi} style={{marginBottom:8}}>\n        <div onClick={()=>setOpen(open===pi?-1:pi)} style={{display:\"flex\",alignItems:\"center\",gap:12,padding:\"10px 14px\",background:open===pi?`${p.c}10`:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.02)\",border:`1px solid ${open===pi?p.c:C.border}`,borderRadius:4,cursor:\"pointer\",userSelect:\"none\"}}>\n          <div style={{width:8,height:8,background:p.c,borderRadius:\"50%\",flexShrink:0}}/>\n          <span style={{color:p.c,fontFamily:M,fontSize:12,fontWeight:\"bold\",letterSpacing:\"0.06em\"}}>{p.phase}</span>\n          <span style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,marginLeft:\"auto\"}}>{p.steps.length} steps \u00b7 click to {open===pi?\"collapse\":\"expand\"}</span>\n        </div>\n        {open===pi&&(\n          <div style={{border:`1px solid ${p.c}33`,borderTop:\"none\",borderRadius:\"0 0 4px 4px\",background:`${p.c}05`,padding:\"12px 14px\"}}>\n            {p.steps.map((s,si)=>(\n              <div key={si} style={{display:\"flex\",gap:14,padding:\"10px 0\",borderBottom:si<p.steps.length-1?\"1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)\":\"none\"}}>\n                <div style={{display:\"flex\",flexDirection:\"column\",alignItems:\"center\",gap:4,flexShrink:0}}>\n                  <div style={{width:24,height:24,background:p.c,borderRadius:\"50%\",display:\"flex\",alignItems:\"center\",justifyContent:\"center\",color:\"#000\",fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>{si+1}</div>\n                  {si<p.steps.length-1&&<div style={{width:1,flex:1,background:`${p.c}30`}}/>}\n                </div>\n                <div style={{flex:1,paddingTop:2}}>\n                  <div style={{color:p.c,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,fontWeight:\"bold\",marginBottom:4}}>{s.t}</div>\n                  <div style={{color:C.dim,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,lineHeight:1.75}}>{s.d}</div>\n                </div>\n              </div>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n    ))}\n    <div style={{marginTop:20,padding:\"14px 16px\",border:`1px solid rgba(255,230,0,0.3)`,background:\"rgba(255,230,0,0.04)\",borderRadius:4}}>\n      <div style={{color:C.yellow,fontFamily:M,fontSize:12,fontWeight:\"bold\",letterSpacing:\"0.06em\",marginBottom:10}}>\u26a0 CRITICAL SAFETY REMINDERS</div>\n      {[\"ALWAYS remove props/fan guards before any programming or calibration session. Treat every EDF as dangerous even when 'not armed'.\",\n        \"Never exceed T/W 2.0 minimum. Always calculate actual AUW before flight with current battery and payload.\",\n        \"GPS lock (HDOP \u22641.5, \u22656 sats) is REQUIRED before transition to cruise. Do not transition below 8m AGL.\",\n        \"LiPo fire: keep a Class D fire extinguisher or LiPo safe bag on site. Charge in a fireproof bag, never unattended.\",\n        \"Part 107 (US): aircraft >250g requires registration. Night ops require anti-collision lights (already installed). Maintain VLOS at all times.\",\n        \"CAN bus: always verify termination before flight. A missing 120\u03a9 terminator causes data corruption that may result in loss of CM4 control link.\",\n      ].map((s,i)=>(<div key={i} style={{display:\"flex\",gap:10,padding:\"4px VariableNozzleDiagram(){\n  const VW=480, VH=320;\n  const CX=240, CY=160, ID=SC(20), OD=SC(32); // 40mm ID \u2192 32mm hull-scale\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" })}\n      {/* Inner rotating ring */}\n      <circle cx={CX} cy={CY} r={OD} fill=\"rgba(45,212,191,0.08)\" stroke={C.teal} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      <circle cx={CX} cy={CY} r={ID} closed; adjust pushrod length\"/>\n        <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"The variable nozzle is not required for flight \u2014 a fixed nozzle at 100% area works fine. The variable nozzle adds ~15% more useful thrust range and meaningfully improves cruise Isp at speeds above 18 m/s. NavLightDiagram(){\n  const VW=720,VH=360,CY=180;\n  function arc(cx,cy,r,a1,a2,col){\n    const s=a1*Math.PI/180,e=a2*Math.PI/180;\n    const x1=cx+r*Math.cos(s),y1=cy+r*Math.sin(s),x2=cx+r*Math.cos(e),y2=cy+r*Math.sin(e);\n    return<path d={`M${cx},${cy} L${x1},${y1} A${r},${r} 0 ${a2-a1>180?1:0},1 ${x2},${y2} Z`} fill={`${col}18`} stroke={col} strokeWidth={0.8} opacity={0.8}/>;\n  }\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n LAYER_COL={Core:C.accent,AHRS:C.teal,Control:C.orange,Protocol:C.orange,Driver:C.teal,Algorithm:C.pink,OS:C.green,Middleware:C.purple,Security:C.yellow,Logging:C.lime,Comms:C.accent,App:C.green,Library:C.purple};\n\nfunction SbomTab(){\n  const [sf,setSf]=useState(\"All\");\n  const rows=sf===\"All\"?SBOM:SBOM.filter(s=>s.sys===sf);\n  return(<div>\n    <div style={{display:\"flex\",gap:5,flexWrap:\"wrap\",marginBottom:12}}>\n      {[\"All\",...SBOM_SYS].map(s=>(<button key={s} onClick={()=>setSf(s)} style={{background:sf===s?\"rgba(0,229,255,0.09)\":\"transparent\",border:`1px solid ${sf===s?C.accent:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.14)\"}`,color:sf===s?C.accent:C.dimmer,padding:\"3px 10px\",fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,cursor:\"pointer\",borderRadius:2}}>{s}</button>))}\n    </div>\n    <div style={{overflowX:\"auto\"}}>\n      <table 2\"?C.accent:s.sys===\"CM4\"?C.green:C.orange,fontWeight:\"bold\",whiteSpace:\"nowrap\",fontSize:9}}>{s.sys}</td>\n          <td style={{padding:\"5px 8px\"}}><span
