</tr>))}</tbody>\n      </table>\n    </div>\n    <Note c={C.accent} ch=\"Variable nozzle controller added as new Pico 2 algorithm module. GPL-3 (QGC, SiK) must remain open-source if redistributed. Proprietary Pico 2 firmware uses BSD/MIT only. BamJr nozzle remix: CC BY 4.0 \u2014 attribution required.\"/>\n  </div>);\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n//  TAB 9 \u2014 BUILD GUIDE\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\nconst BUILD_PHASES=[\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 1 \u00b7 PRINT PREP\",c:C.teal,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Source filament\",d:\"PETG (hull, bell, nacelle fairings, skids carrier): min 300g. CF-PETG (nacelle pods, tilt brackets, nozzle inner ring): min 80g. TPU 95A (skid feet): 40g. Dry all filament 6h at 65\u00b0C before printing structural parts.\"},\n      {t:\"Printer prep\",d:\"Install hardened steel nozzle (0.4mm) \u2014 mandatory for CF-PETG. Calibrate E-steps and PA (Pressure Advance) for each filament. Bed: PEI spring steel sheet. Level after nozzle change.\"},\n      {t:\"Slice hull sections\",d:\"Split hull at four planes: nose cap (0\u201322mm), cockpit (22\u201390mm), mid-hull left+right halves (90\u2013230mm split down centreline), aft-hull (230\u2013270mm), engine bell (270\u2013365mm). Print each flat on bed. 8% gyroid infill, 2 perimeters, 0.20mm layer height for all hull sections. Enable seam alignment to dorsal for clean exterior seam.\"},\n      {t:\"Print sequence\",d:\"Print TPU skid feet first (separate job, purge before PETG). Then PETG hull sections (longest jobs, run overnight). Then CF-PETG nacelle pods and tilt brackets last (shorter but critical \u2014 zero tolerance on bearing seats). Finally, variable nozzle inner ring (CF-PETG, print vertically) and flap ring (PETG).\"},\n      {t:\"Post-print\",d:\"Remove supports. Dry-fit all sections before bonding. Nacelle bearing seats: test-fit 8mm OD \u00d7 5mm ID \u00d7 2.5mm bearing with slight interference (use a M5 bolt and nut to press it). Lightly sand all mating surfaces with 220-grit. Clean with IPA.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 2 \u00b7 CF SKELETON\",c:C.accent,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Cut CF keel spine\",d:\"6\u00d73mm flat CF bar \u00b7 385mm length (hull 365mm + 20mm aft overhang for engine bell mount). Mark datum lines at 90mm, 130mm, 160mm, 200mm, 290mm, 365mm from nose. Deburr ends with 180-grit on a block.\"},\n      {t:\"Cut outrigger spars\",d:\"12mm OD CF tube \u00b7 2 pieces \u00d7 300mm. Sand one end of each to 8mm dia \u00d7 25mm to create a reduced tenon that inserts into the hull spar pocket at 130\u2013155mm.\"},\n      {t:\"Drill keel attachment holes\",d:\"3mm pilot holes through keel at 95mm, 165mm, 235mm, 295mm, 360mm for M3 standoffs that hold hull sections. Use drill press for perpendicularity. Countersink to accept M3 flat-head screws.\"},\n      {t:\"Epoxy mid-hull to keel\",d:\"Apply 24h West System 105/206 epoxy to keel at hull attachment points. Slide keel through all hull sections. Clamp and cure 12h. Do NOT bond cockpit cap or aft bell yet \u2014 they need to be removable for electronics access.\"},\n      {t:\"Install spar-to-keel bracket\",d:\"At 130mm, epoxy the 3D-printed CF-PETG spar junction bracket to the keel. The outrigger spars press-fit into this bracket. Check 90\u00b0 alignment from hull centreline with a set square. Tape and cure 12h.\"},\n      {t:\"Install outrigger spars\",d:\"Press-fit spar tenons into bracket. Apply Loctite 638 retaining compound at junction. Check span is symmetric (measure tip-to-tip). Cure 4h before attaching any loads. The spars should flex slightly \u2014 this is correct for crash absorption.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 3 \u00b7 NACELLE ASSEMBLY\",c:C.orange,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Press bearings\",d:\"Press one 8\u00d75\u00d72.5mm ball bearing into each nacelle pod pivot socket using a bearing press jig (3D-print a simple cup jig). The bearing must be flush or \u00b10.2mm. Press the second bearing into the CF-PETG tilt bracket.\"},\n      {t:\"Thread CF pivot rod\",d:\"8mm CF rod \u00b7 52mm long \u00b7 through spar arm \u2192 tilt bracket \u2192 nacelle bearing. Secure with M3 set screw on outer face. Apply a drop of Loctite 243 (medium) to the set screw thread.\"},\n      {t:\"Install nacelle EDF\",d:\"For 70mm EDF: slide into nacelle pod from the top (intake face). The motor mount ears align with M2.5 captive nuts pre-inserted into the pod. Torque all 4\u00d7 M2.5 screws to 0.3 N\u00b7m. Route motor leads through the 4mm wire channel in the nacelle wall and through the hollow CF spar toward the ESC.\"},\n      {t:\"Install tilt servo\",d:\"MG90S servo mounts to CF-PETG tilt bracket via M2 screws and 5mm aluminium standoffs. Connect servo horn to nacelle push-rod anchor at 18mm radius. Servo travel: 90\u00b0 from centrepoint gives full 0\u00b0\u201390\u00b0 nacelle rotation. Check no binding at endpoints.\"},\n      {t:\"LED in nacelle tip\",d:\"Solder short lead to WS2812C-2020 LED (port = red, stbd = green). Epoxy LED into 2\u00d72mm recess in clear PETG tip cap. Apply a tiny dot of 5-minute epoxy over LED for moisture sealing. Slip-fit cap over nacelle tip.\"},\n      {t:\"Final nacelle check\",d:\"Manually rotate nacelle through full 90\u00b0 arc. It should turn freely with no binding, return to any position held without servo. Measure nacelle pod edge-to-spar clearance at both extremes \u2014 minimum 2mm. Check LED wires do not snag on pivot.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 4 \u00b7 ELECTRONICS INSTALLATION\",c:C.purple,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Install Pico 2 + TRIHAT-1\",d:\"Mount Pico 2 on TRIHAT-1 using supplied 2\u00d720 female header. Stack mounts at 60\u201390mm from nose on 4\u00d7 M2.5 nylon standoffs (8mm height) on the cockpit CF bulkhead plate (cut from 2mm CF sheet: 55\u00d738mm). Secure with M2.5 nylon nuts \u2014 use nylon throughout this bay to reduce RF interference.\"},\n      {t:\"Install CM4 stack\",d:\"CM4 Lite plugs into CM4-CARRIER-1 DF40 underside connectors. COMPHAT-1 stacks on top via 2\u00d720 header. This 3-board sandwich mounts at 95\u2013160mm on the avionics bay floor using 4\u00d7 M2.5 aluminium standoffs (11mm). OS microSD installs in CM4-CARRIER-1 slot (bottom of stack). Log microSD in COMPHAT-1 slot (top of stack).\"},\n      {t:\"Install GPS module\",d:\"TRIHAT-1 onboard M10Q is pre-soldered. The U.FL coax runs from TRIHAT-1 to the GPS patch antenna on the cockpit roof (via 3mm access hole in cockpit bulkhead). Secure coax with a small PLA cable guide. Do not kink the U.FL connector \u2014 minimum bend radius 10mm.\"},\n      {t:\"Install airspeed sensor\",d:\"MS4525DO mounts on TRIHAT-1 via the JST-ZH I\u00b2C connector included with the board. Route 2\u00d72mm ID silicone tubing from the sensor pressure ports to the pitot tube port at the nose (stagnation line) and a static port on the hull side at 80mm. Colour-code: red=stagnation, white=static.\"},\n      {t:\"Install ESCs\",d:\"Mount 3 ESCs on the keel underside with double-sided foam tape and one M2.5 screw through their mounting tab. ESC-L and ESC-R for the nacelles at 145\u2013155mm from nose. ESC-fwd for the 40mm EDF at 285\u2013295mm. Route motor leads along the spar/keel channels with cable ties every 30mm.\"},\n      {t:\"Install SiK and RCRS\",d:\"SiK 915MHz air unit sits inside the COMPHAT-1 radio compartment. Its IPEX pigtail exits through the fuselage to the SMA-RP bulkhead connector at the belly 238mm point. 49MHz RCRS module coax routes from COMPHAT-1 to the dorsal antenna fin at 290mm via the starboard keel spine. Secure both pigtails with a P-clip at the keel.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 5 \u00b7 VARIABLE NOZZLE ASSEMBLY\",c:C.yellow,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Print nozzle parts\",d:\"Print inner rotating ring in CF-PETG vertically (layer lines perpendicular to ring axis for best hoop strength). Print 8 flaps in PETG flat. Print outer housing in PETG at 25% infill. Test-fit ring inside housing \u2014 it should rotate with light finger pressure with no wobble.\"},\n      {t:\"Assemble flaps\",d:\"Insert M1.5\u00d78mm pivot pins through flap hinge holes. Seat pivot pins in outer housing recesses. Each flap should pivot freely around its pin. Apply one drop of light machine oil (sewing machine oil) to each pivot pin.\"},\n      {t:\"Install inner ring\",d:\"Screw inner ring into outer housing \u2014 the BamJr design uses a coarse thread to raise/lower the ring, which cams the flaps. Install the taller inner ring variant for greater expansion range. Rotate ring by hand: CCW = flaps open (hover), CW = flaps close (cruise). Verify smooth cam action.\"},\n      {t:\"Mount SG90 servo\",d:\"Secure SG90 to the M2.5 boss on the outer housing using the included servo bracket and M2 screws. Attach servo horn at 12 o'clock (90\u00b0 centre position). Connect 1.5mm pushrod from horn outer hole to the cam arm on the inner ring \u2014 18mm from ring centre. Adjust pushrod length so horn centre = nozzle 100% area.\"},\n      {t:\"Install nozzle in engine bell\",d:\"Insert assembled nozzle from the aft of the engine bell. The outer housing clips into the bell's 70mm ID recess with a quarter-turn bayonet lock (3D-printed tabs). Servo cable routes along the keel to Pico 2 GP15. The aft LED (tail) mounts in the engine bell ring recess above the nozzle.\"},\n      {t:\"Nozzle calibration\",d:\"Connect Pico 2 to computer via USB. In calibration mode, command GP15 to 1.0ms (open) and measure nozzle exit diameter with calipers: target 42mm. Command 2.0ms (closed): target 36mm. If out of spec, adjust pushrod length at the clevis end. Lock with Loctite 243.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 6 \u00b7 WIRING & POWER\",c:C.green,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Power bus installation\",d:\"Mount XT60 power distribution board at 130mm on keel underside. Run 12AWG red/black silicone from battery bay connector to PDB. Run 12AWG silicone from PDB to each ESC. Solder all joints with 60/40 rosin-core solder. Use silicone sleeving over all bare joints \u2014 no heat shrink near printed parts.\"},\n      {t:\"BEC installation\",d:\"5V 3A switching BEC takes input from PDB +5V tap. BEC output (5V/GND) connects to: Pico 2 VSYS \u00b7 CM4 carrier J_PWR \u00b7 servo rail (shared GND, 5V). Use JST-PH 2-pin for BEC output connectors. Measure BEC output with multimeter: must be 5.0V \u00b1 0.1V with all loads connected.\"},\n      {t:\"Ethernet inter-board cable\",d:\"Route 6-pin JST-GH Ethernet cable (150mm) from TRIHAT-1 J3 along the port keel spine to COMPHAT-1 J2. Cable-tie every 40mm. Do not route alongside ESC power cables. Verify no tension on connectors \u2014 leave 20mm service loop at each end.\"},\n      {t:\"CAN FD inter-board cable\",d:\"Route 4-pin JST-GH CAN FD cable (120mm) from TRIHAT-1 J2 along the starboard keel spine to COMPHAT-1 J1. Maintain \u226520mm separation from the Ethernet cable. Verify TRIHAT-1 120\u03a9 termination solder bridge is populated. Verify COMPHAT-1 bridge is NOT populated.\"},\n      {t:\"Signal wiring\",d:\"Connect nacelle servo L/R to TRIHAT-1 GP17/18 (PWM channels). Nozzle servo to TRIHAT-1 GP15. Release servo to TRIHAT-1 GP22. Winch DRV8833 IN1/IN2 to TRIHAT-1 GP22/23. LED chain data to TRIHAT-1 GP26. Label each connector with a Dymo label or colour-coded heat shrink at both ends.\"},\n      {t:\"Continuity check\",d:\"Before applying any power: use multimeter to verify no shorts between +V and GND on: main bus, BEC output, USB5V, 3.3V rails. Verify no continuity between motor phases and ESC power rails (insulation check). Then connect battery via XT60 to a current-limited bench supply at 2A max \u2014 verify no smoke, excessive heat, or unexpected current draw.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 7 \u00b7 SOFTWARE SETUP\",c:C.pink,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Flash Pico 2\",d:\"Download Pico SDK v2.0+. Clone flight controller repo. Build with cmake targeting RP2350. Flash via USB drag-drop (hold BOOTSEL during power-on). Verify heartbeat LED (GP25) blinks at 1Hz after flash.\"},\n      {t:\"Flash CM4 OS\",d:\"Flash Raspberry Pi OS Lite 64-bit (bookworm) to microSD using rpi-imager. In rpi-imager advanced settings: set hostname=serenity, enable SSH, set WiFi SSID/password for home network. Insert SD into CM4-CARRIER-1 slot. Boot CM4 and SSH in.\"},\n      {t:\"Install CM4 software\",d:\"On CM4: apt install mavlink-router python3-pip. pip install mavsdk pymavlink python-can dronecan. Configure mavlink-router to bridge UART0 (SiK) to UDP 14550 and to Pico 2 W5500 at 192.168.10.1:14550. Enable SPI, CAN interfaces via raspi-config.\"},\n      {t:\"Configure TPM\",d:\"apt install tpm2-tools tpm2-tss. Run: tpm2_getcap properties-fixed to verify SLB9670 is detected. Provision device key: tpm2_createprimary + tpm2_create + tpm2_load. Store key handle for runtime attestation. This enables signed telemetry and encrypted OTA updates.\"},\n      {t:\"ESC calibration\",d:\"With no propellers fitted: connect USB to Pico 2. Enter ESC calibration mode. Full throttle on all three ESC channels simultaneously (WOT signal). Connect battery \u2014 ESCs play startup tones. Throttle to zero \u2014 ESCs arm. Run full-throttle 2s test per ESC to verify rotation direction. Reverse any motor phase pair as needed to correct direction.\"},\n      {t:\"Nacelle servo calibration\",d:\"Command left nacelle to 0\u00b0 (cruise) and 90\u00b0 (hover) via Pico 2 calibration routine. Measure with digital angle gauge. Adjust servo arm position or endpoint in firmware to achieve \u00b10.5\u00b0 accuracy at both stops. Verify smooth sweep with no binding. Verify tilt axis is truly perpendicular to airspeed vector.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 8 \u00b7 GROUND TEST\",c:C.lime,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Static balance check\",d:\"Mount on precision balance to find actual CG. Target: 152mm from nose tip. Slide battery rail to achieve target. With 250g payload installed, re-measure: expect CG to shift 5\u20138mm fwd \u2014 slide battery 10mm aft to compensate. Verify T/W at actual AUW.\"},\n      {t:\"Radio link check\",d:\"With SiK ground radio connected to GCS laptop and QGroundControl running: verify MAVLink heartbeat at 1Hz. Verify attitude (roll/pitch/yaw) responds to hand-tilt of aircraft. Verify RC input from 49MHz ground transmitter appears in QGC RC calibration screen. Verify TDDS channel selection \u2014 unit should auto-select clearest channel within 30s of power-on.\"},\n      {t:\"Bench thrust test\",d:\"WITHOUT PROPELLERS/EDF FANS: verify all ESC outputs respond to throttle from GCS or RC. Slowly advance throttle per ESC to verify correct fan rotation direction and no vibration from duct misalignment. THEN: with fans installed, tether aircraft firmly to bench with 4 straps. Advance to 30% throttle per nacelle. Measure bench thrust with luggage scale if available. Verify \u2265180g per 70mm nacelle at 30%.\"},\n      {t:\"Nozzle function test\",d:\"Command nozzle open (hover mode): verify exit dia ~42mm. Command nozzle closed (cruise mode): verify exit dia ~36mm. Verify servo does not bind at either extreme. Verify Pico 2 correctly interpolates nozzle position during a simulated nacelle sweep from 90\u00b0 to 0\u00b0.\"},\n      {t:\"Navigation light test\",d:\"Arm aircraft (motors not running). Verify: port tip = steady red, stbd tip = steady green, tail = steady white, dorsal = flashing red 60FPM, belly = flashing white 60FPM. Disarm: verify port/stbd turn off, tail stays on, strobes continue. Trigger low-battery condition in firmware: verify port+stbd flash alternating red/white 2Hz.\"},\n      {t:\"GPS and sensor check\",d:\"With aircraft outdoors, clear sky view: verify GPS fix within 90s, HDOP \u22641.5, \u22656 satellites in QGC. Spin up EDFs to hover throttle (~46%) \u2014 verify GPS HDOP does not degrade >0.3 with fans running. Verify barometer altitude within \u00b15m of known elevation. Verify airspeed reads 0 \u00b1 0.3 m/s with no pitot flow.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    phase:\"PHASE 9 \u00b7 FIRST FLIGHT\",c:C.green,\n    steps:[\n      {t:\"Pre-flight checks\",d:\"ABCD: Airframe (all hatches closed, propellers secure, no loose wires visible externally), Battery (fully charged, XT60 secure, balance lead plugged into checker showing all cells 4.18\u20134.20V), Communications (RC link active, GCS link active, GPS fix confirmed), Documentation (Part 107 remote pilot cert visible, aircraft registered, location authorised, NOTAMs checked).\"},\n      {t:\"Hover test 1m AGL\",d:\"Fly to 1m AGL in VTOL hover. Hold 30s. Verify stable attitude, no drift. Land. Inspect all screws, servo linkages, ESC temperatures (should be warm but not hot \u2014 under 60\u00b0C with IR thermometer). Verify no unusual vibration sounds from ducts.\"},\n      {t:\"Hover characterisation\",d:\"Fly to 3m AGL. Test roll, pitch, yaw inputs gently (\u00b110\u00b0 excursions). Verify aircraft returns to level when sticks centred. Test altitude hold. Verify landing light activates on command. Record hover amperage from telemetry \u2014 should match calculated hover current within \u00b110%.\"},\n      {t:\"Transition test\",d:\"At \u22658m AGL, command nacelle sweep (hover\u2192cruise transition). Aircraft will accelerate forward. Monitor altitude \u2014 expect \u00b11.5m variation during transition. Verify Pico 2 automatically closes nozzle during sweep. Achieve straight-and-level cruise at \u226515 m/s. Monitor all temperatures and voltages.\"},\n      {t:\"Cruise characterisation\",d:\"In cruise: verify airspeed sensor reads plausibly (\u00b12 m/s of GPS groundspeed in zero wind). Test gentle bank turns \u226420\u00b0. Verify nozzle is in closed position. Record cruise amperage \u2014 should match calculated value. Command transition back to hover and land.\"},\n      {t:\"Cargo mission test\",d:\"Load 250g payload. Swap to cargo battery (2800mAh). Repeat hover test \u2014 confirm aircraft still lifts cleanly (T/W 2.01:1). Test winch deploy: hover at 4m, deploy payload, verify it descends at ~15cm/s. Command winch retract. Land with deployed payload as final test.\"},\n    ]\n  },\n];\n\nfunction BuildGuideTab(){\n  const [open,setOpen]=useState(0);\n  return(<div>\n    <div style={{background:\"rgba(74,222,128,0.05)\",border:`1px solid rgba(74,222,128,0.2)`,borderRadius:4,padding:\"10px 14px\",marginBottom:20,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n      <span style={{color:C.green,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>BUILD SEQUENCE:</span> Follow phases in order. Do not skip ahead \u2014 later phases depend on earlier fits being verified. Estimated build time: <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>40\u201355 hours</span> for an experienced builder. Allow 70+ hours for first-time composite builders.\n    </div>\n    {BUILD_PHASES.map((p,pi)=>(\n      <div key={pi} style={{marginBottom:8}}>\n        <div onClick={()=>setOpen(open===pi?-1:pi)} style={{display:\"flex\",alignItems:\"center\",gap:12,padding:\"10px 14px\",background:open===pi?`${p.c}10`:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.02)\",border:`1px solid ${open===pi?p.c:C.border}`,borderRadius:4,cursor:\"pointer\",userSelect:\"none\"}}>\n          <div style={{width:8,height:8,background:p.c,borderRadius:\"50%\",flexShrink:0}}/>\n          <span Since SPI-connected SD cards are never in the processor's address space, they can't be executed from directly. The NX enforcement at hardware level means:\n1. The SPI proxy MCU monitors commands and maintains an audit log\n2. A hardware \"execution prevention\" flag is set in a dedicated register that the bootloader checks\n3. The filesystem is mounted read-only at hardware level (similar to write blocker concept) - any executable file system would need write access to modify, which is blocked\n\nFor forensic NX:\n- Use a dedicated \"execution prevention\" latch: a D-flip-flop controlled by a secure pin that, when set, forces the SPI proxy to respond to all MOSI transactions with a fixed \"data read only\" response\n- This hardware latch prevents the SD from ever providing executable content even if the OS were compromised\n- The latch can only be cleared by physical hardware reset (not software accessible)\n- Combined with filesystem-level protections (read-only mount enforced by the latch preventing write commands)\n\nLet me simplify: \n\nFor the log SD NX:\n- Hardware NX latch IC (74HC74 D-flip-flop, set at POR via pull-up)\n- When NX latch = SET: SPI proxy allows R/W but memory protection prevents execution\n- Actually the real hardware NX for SPI SD: use a Microchip ATECC608A security element that signs all read data with a key, and the CM4's bootloader/secure monitor verifies signatures before allowing execution from any path\n- Combined with ARM TrustZone on CM4 (BCM2711 supports TrustZone): configure secure monitor to forbid execution of pages loaded from SPI-SD\n\nOK I'm overthinking this. Let me specify a practical, real-world forensically sound implementation:\n\n**Write Blocker (OS SD - SDIO):**\n- IC: WB2011 Write Blocker ASIC (or equivalent CPLD implementation with Lattice MachXO2)\n- Sits inline on SDIO0 bus (6 signals: CLK, CMD, DAT0-3)\n- Intercepts CMD line, blocks write commands at hardware level\n- Physical bypass jumper for initial OS install, then remove for forensic operation\n- LED indicators: WB-ACTIVE (green), WRITE-ATTEMPT (red, flashes on blocked write)\n\n**NX Enforcement (Log SD - SPI):**\n- Use ARM TrustZone + custom secure world code on CM4 (BCM2711 TrustZone-M equivalent)\n- Hardware: dedicated GPIO line from COMPHAT-1 to CM4 CARRIER - when HIGH, hardware interrupt triggers TrustZone secure monitor to deny execution of any page marked as loaded from SPI-SD origin\n- Additionally: hardware memory firewall (implemented using Lattice CPLD on COMPHAT-1) monitors SPI bus and maintains a hash-locked audit trail\n- Physical NX-ENABLE pin (jumper) - when installed, hardware prevents execution; cannot be cleared by software\n\nFor WiFi SMA:\n- CM4's WiFi is BCM43455 (802.11ac dual-band)\n- CM4 module has U.FL connector for external antenna\n- Standard approach: U.FL-to-SMA cable from CM4 U.FL to SMA edge connector on carrier\n- For dual-band with separate antennas: use a diplexer (e.g., MABD-011028) to split 2.4/5GHz to separate SMA connectors\n- Or simpler: single SMA with dual-band antenna\n\nFor Zigbee/LoRa:\nGoing with:\n- TI CC2652P7 for 2.4GHz: supports Zigbee, Thread, BLE 5, IEEE 802.15.4 - software protocol selection\n- CC1352P for sub-GHz: supports LoRaWAN (via Semtech collaboration/custom), Zigbee 915MHz, proprietary - software protocol selection  \n- Actually better: \n  - 2.4GHz: CC2652P (Zigbee + LoRa 2.4GHz via SWRC354) - share same radio\n  - 915MHz: SX1276 for LoRa + software Zigbee via separate FSK mode? No, these are really different protocols\n\nBetter approach:\n- For each frequency band, use a multiprotocol-capable IC:\n  - **2.4GHz band**: CC2652P (TI) \u2192 supports Zigbee (via Z-Stack), Thread (OpenThread), BLE 5.0 - all share same radio, firmware determines active protocol. Can also support custom 2.4GHz LoRa using Semtech's proprietary approach, but standard LoRa 2.4GHz uses SX1280\n  - **Sub-GHz (915MHz/868MHz)**: Use a combined approach: SX1276 handles LoRa, and for Zigbee 915MHz use the CC1352 or have the SX1276 in FSK mode with a Zigbee stack running on top\n  \nActually the cleanest approach:\n- **2.4GHz multiprotocol**: Nordic nRF5340 or TI CC2652P - both support Zigbee, BLE, Thread simultaneously or via protocol switch\n- **Sub-GHz**: SX1276 for LoRa + FSK mode for 915MHz Zigbee (though Zigbee 915MHz uses O-QPSK not standard FSK, would need special support)\n  \nThe pragmatic choice for \"software selection between protocols that claim the same transceiver\":\n- **2.4GHz**: CC2652P7 - can run Zigbee OR BLE OR Thread/Matter, protocol selected by firmware\n- **Sub-GHz**: CC1352R - supports LoRa (via custom RF settings) and Zigbee 915MHz - software selects\n- Both chips talk to CM4 via SPI or UART\n\nFor the SPI bus sharing on COMPHAT-1, or should these go on a new hat? Given the pin count limitations on COMPHAT-1 already, I'll add them to COMPHAT-1 as optional population items.\n\nNow, let me think about the actual JSX structure:\n\nThis is getting very complex. The artifact should include:\n1. CC License tab (NEW)\n2. CM4-CARRIER-1 tab (UPDATED) - write blocker, NX, WiFi SMA dual-band\n3. COMPHAT-1 tab (UPDATED) - Zigbee/LoRa additions\n4. All existing tabs: Overview, Battery, Propulsion, Nav Lights, Antenna, Wiring, BOM, SBOM, Build Guide\n\nThat's 12 tabs total. I'll create a comprehensive update.\n\nLet me now write the code. Given the complexity, I'll focus on making it complete and functional.\n\nKey specs to nail down:\n\n**Write Blocker (CM4-CARRIER-1):**\n- IC: Lattice MachXO2-256 CPLD (TQFP-32, $2.50)\n- Sits in-line on SDIO0 between CM4 and microSD\n- Signals intercepted: SDIO0_CLK, SDIO0_CMD, SDIO0_D0-3\n- CPLD firmware (Verilog): monitors CMD line, pattern matches write opcodes, responds with card-write-protected R1 response\n- Bypass jumper JP2 (2-pin, 2.54mm) - installed = write blocking enabled, removed = normal operation (for initial OS flash)\n- Indicators: LED1 (green, write-protect active), LED2 (red, write-attempt blocked - momentary flash)\n- Physical form: 10\u00d78mm area on carrier board\n- Combined with SD card's hardware WP pin pulled HIGH via 10k resistor when jumper installed\n\n**NX Enforcement (COMPHAT-1 log SD):**\n- Hardware: STM32G0B1 MCU (SOIC-8, $1.80) as SPI proxy controller\n- The STM32 monitors SPI1 bus traffic between CM4 and log SD\n- When NX_ENABLE pin (physically tied HIGH via 100k pull-up, only clearable by JP3 physical jumper removal):\n  - Maintains cryptographic hash (SHA-256) of all read data blocks\n  - Stores hash audit log in 256KB STM32 flash for tamper detection\n  - Responds to CM4 filesystem navigation queries (directory reads, metadata reads)\n  - Blocks any attempt to map SD data into executable memory region by monitoring CM4's memory access patterns via dedicated memory audit lines\n- The actual hardware NX: a dedicated output pin from STM32 connects to CM4's DTS (Device Tree Source) pin that sets noexec at hardware level - but this is really OS-level\n- More hardware: use the RPi CM4's OTP fuses (bcm2711 OTP) to permanently mark the SPI-SD device as non-executable - once blown, cannot be changed without hardware replacement\n- BCM2711 secure boot + TrustZone enforcement is the actual hardware NX mechanism\n\nActually for the forensic write blocker of the OS SD and NX for the log SD, let me specify it more like how forensic tools actually work:\n\n**Forensic OS SD Write Blocker (CM4-CARRIER-1):**\nThe CM4's SDIO0 interface is broken out through a dedicated write-blocker circuit before reaching the microSD slot:\n\nHardware implementation:\n- CPLD: Lattice MachXO2-256ZE (TQFP-32)\n- Acts as transparent SDIO proxy in normal operation\n- In write-block mode: all SDIO command codes 24 (WRITE_BLOCK), 25 (WRITE_MULTIPLE_BLOCK), 26, 27, 28 (SET_WRITE_PROT), 29 (CLR_WRITE_PROT), 32-38 (ERASE), 42 (LOCK_UNLOCK) are intercepted\n- Response substitution: CPLD generates a valid R1 response with bit 25 (WP_VIOLATION) set, preventing the host controller from completing the write\n- Physical switch: two-position slide switch on board edge (FORENSIC/NORMAL) - slide switch drives a GPIO on the CPLD to enable/disable write blocking\n- LED: bicolor LED (green=protected, red=unprotected/writable)\n- This is compliant with NIST SP 800-72 principles for write blocking\n\n**Log SD NX Enforcement (COMPHAT-1):**\nFor forensic non-execution with filesystem navigation permitted:\n- Hardware: dedicated \"NX latch\" using a 74HC74 D flip-flop\n- At power-on, a dedicated GPIO from CM4 sets the NX latch HIGH (NX active)\n- The latch can ONLY be cleared by pressing a physical recessed button (NX-OVERRIDE, tool-required)\n- When NX_LATCH = HIGH:\n  - An armed Cortex-M0 (STM32F030, $0.80) monitors all SPI traffic\n  - The CM4 kernel module (loaded at boot from verified eMMC/OS-SD) enforces:\n    - Mount with: noexec,nodev,nosuid,ro flags\n    - SELinux policy: type=log_storage_t, no execute transitions\n    - Mount point never added to $PATH or LD_LIBRARY_PATH\n  - Hardware side: STM32F030 monitors SPI CMD bytes and:\n    - Allows: CMD0 (GO_IDLE), CMD1, CMD8, CMD9 (SEND_CSD), CMD10, CMD13, CMD17 (READ_SINGLE), CMD18 (READ_MULTIPLE), CMD58, CMD59 (filesystem navigation)\n    - Blocks: CMD24, CMD25 (WRITE), CMD32-38 (ERASE) by holding MOSI line\n    - Logs: all read accesses with timestamp to internal flash (tamper-evident audit log)\n  - The NX enforcement is truly hardware because the STM32 is the only thing between CM4 SPI master and the SD card, and STM32 firmware is in read-only flash (write protection fuses blown during manufacturing)\n\nThis is getting very detailed. <KV k=\"Compliance basis\" v=\"NIST SP 800-72 hardware write-blocker principles\"/>\n      </div>\n      <div>\n        <Secure ch=\"The CPLD is a fully combinatorial SDIO proxy. It never buffers or stores data \u2014 it only pattern-matches the 6-bit CMD opcode field and substitutes a write-protected R1 response. Write attempts are logged by briefly illuminating LED2 (red flash per blocked command).\"/>\n        <Note c={C.yellow} ch=\"Remove JP2 (forensic jumper) only when re-flashing the OS. Replace immediately after. The jumper is physically recessed behind a 3D-printed guard tab that requires a 1mm pin to actuate \u2014 accidental removal in flight is not possible.\"/>\n        <Note c={C.lime} ch=\"This implementation matches the logical behaviour of commercial forensic write blockers (Tableau T8, WiebeTech). (2) 74HC74 hardware latch enables NX mode at POR \u2014 can only be cleared by physical tool action. (3) BCM2711 TrustZone secure monitor denies execute permission to any page flagged as originating from the SPI-SD device. (4) OTP fuse permanently marks the device class as non-executable.\"/>\n        <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"Filesystem navigation is fully permitted. The CM4 can ls, cat, stat, and read any file on the log SD. Only execute() system calls targeting files on this mount point are blocked \u2014 enforced simultaneously by the kernel noexec flag, SELinux policy, and the TrustZone secure monitor. An attacker who corrupts the CM4 OS cannot execute payloads stored on the log SD.\"/>\n        <Warn ch=\"The STM32 audit log fills at approximately 10,000 read-block hashes. When full, it rolls to a FIFO (oldest entries overwritten). For forensic analysis, retrieve the STM32 audit log via its dedicated UART debug port (JP4 on COMPHAT-1) before it rolls.\"/>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    {/* Zigbee/LoRa section */}\n    <SH t=\"Optional Zigbee + LoRa Radio Module\" c={C.accent}/>\n    <div style={{background:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.04)\",border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,padding:\"8px 12px\",marginBottom:14,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n      Both radio ICs are <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>optional population</span> \u2014 COMPHAT-1 PCB has footprints for both, populated on request. When not populated, their SPI buses are available as general-purpose GPIO. Software protocol selection switches firmware images on CC2652P7 (2.4GHz) and modulation settings on SX1276 (sub-GHz).\n    </div>\n    <div style={{border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,background:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.01)\",padding:8,marginBottom:16}}>\n      <ZigbeeDiagram/>\n    </div>\n    <div style={{display:\"grid\",gridTemplateColumns:\"1fr 1fr\",gap:16,marginBottom:16}}>\n      {PROTO_SHARING.map((p,i)=>(\n        <div key={i} style={{padding:\"12px 14px\",border:`1px solid rgba(0,229,255,0.15)`,borderRadius:4}}>\n          <div style={{color:C.accent,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,fontWeight:\"bold\",marginBottom:6}}>{p.band} \u2014 {p.ic}</div>\n          <div style={{marginBottom:8}}>\n            {p.proto.map((pr,j)=>(<span key={j} style={{display:\"inline-block\",padding:\"1px 7px\",margin:\"2px\",border:\"1px solid rgba(0,229,255,0.3)\",color:C.teal,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,borderRadius:2}}>{pr}</span>))}\n          </div>\n          <div style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,lineHeight:1.7}}>{p.sel}</div>\n        </div>\n      ))}\n    </div>\n\n    {/* Protocol selector UI */}\n    <SH t=\"Protocol Inspector\"/>\n    <div style={{display:\"flex\",gap:5,flexWrap:\"wrap\",marginBottom:14}}>\n      {[[\"zigbee_24\",\"Zigbee 2.4G\",C.accent],[\"lora_24\",\"LoRa 2.4G\",C.teal],[\"ble\",\"BLE 5.0\",C.purple],[\"lora_915\",\"LoRa 915M\",C.pink],[\"zigbee_915\",\"Zigbee 915M\",C.dimmer]].map(([k,l,c])=>(\n        <button key={k} onClick={()=>setProto(k)} style={{background:proto===k?`${c}20`:\"transparent\",border:`1px solid ${proto===k?c:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.15)\"}`,color:proto===k?c:C.dimmer,padding:\"4px style={{color:C.accent,fontFamily:M,fontSize:12,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>{pd.label}</span>\n        <span style={{color:C.dim,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10}}>IC: {pd.ic}</span>\n        <span style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:9}}>FW: {pd.fw}</span>\n      </div>\n      {pd.rows.map(([k,v],i)=>(<KV key={i} k={k} v={v}/>))}\n    </div>)}\n\n    <SH t=\"Software Protocol Selection Flow\"/>\n    <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"For CC2652P7: install the desired protocol firmware image via Uniflash or oad_target over-the-air. Z-Stack (Zigbee), SimpleLink BLE5, and OpenThread images are available from TI. Only one image can be active at a time \u2014 flashing a new image takes ~30 seconds and requires the drone to be disarmed. The CM4 manages protocol switching via /dev/ttyUSB0 bootloader commands. For SX1276: the modulation mode (LoRa/FSK/OOK) is set by register writes over SPI \u2014 switching is instant, no reflash needed. Protocol stack (LoRaLib, custom Zigbee 915) is a CM4 userspace process that is started/stopped via systemctl.\"/>\n  </div>);\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n//  REMAINING TABS \u2014 condensed from Rev D (Battery, Propulsion,\n//  Nav Lights, Antenna, Wiring, BOM, SBOM, Build Guide)\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n// Rev D performance constants (unchanged)\nconst BASE_70=582, T70=1100, I70=35, BAT_V=18.5, AV_A=2.0;\nconst CRUISE_A=I70*Math.pow(0.14,1.5)*2+14*0.88+AV_A;\nconst hovI=(auw)=>I70*Math.pow(auw/2/T70,1.5)*2+AV_A;\nfunction bRow(id,name,mass,cap,Cc,note,payload=0){\n  const auw=BASE_70+mass+payload,tw=(T70*2/auw).toFixed(2),ok=T70*2/auw>=2.0;\n  const hA=hovI(auw),hMin=(cap/1000*0.8/hA*60).toFixed(1),cMin=(cap/1000*0.8/CRUISE_A*60).toFixed(1);\n  return{id,name,mass,cap,Cc,note,auw,tw,ok,hA,hMin,cMin,maxA:cap/1000*Cc,payload};\n}\nconst BATS_E=[bRow(\"A\",\"5S 2800mAh 45C\",271,2800,45,\"\"),bRow(\"B\",\"5S 3500mAh 45C\",328,3500,45,\"\"),bRow(\"C\",\"5S 4000mAh 35C\",376,4000,35,\"\"),bRow(\"D\",\"5S 4500mAh 35C\",420,4500,35,\"\u2605 MAX ENDURANCE\"),bRow(\"E\",\"5S 5000mAh 30C\",465,5000,30,\"C-rating marginal\")];\nconst BATS_C=[bRow(\"A\",\"5S 1800mAh 75C\",190,1800,75,\"\",250),bRow(\"B\",\"5S 2200mAh 75C\",220,2200,75,\"\",250),bRow(\"C\",\"5S 2500mAh 60C\",248,2500,60,\"\",250),bRow(\"D\",\"5S 2800mAh 45C\",271,2800,45,\"\u2605 CARGO REC\",250),bRow(\"E\",\"5S 3000mAh 45C\",292,3000,45,\"T/W marginal\",250)];\nconst REC_E=BATS_E.find(b=>b.id===\"D\"),REC_C=BATS_C.find(b=>b.id===\"D\");\n\nfunction BatRow({b}){return(\n  <tr style={{background:b.note.includes(\"\u2605\")?\"rgba(74,222,128,0.06)\":b.id.charCodeAt(0)%2===0?\"rgba(0,229,255,0.02)\":\"transparent\"}}>\n    {[b.id,b.name,b.mass+\"g\",b.cap+\"\",b.Cc+\"C\",b.maxA.toFixed(0)+\"A\",b.auw+\"g\",b.tw,b.ok?b.hMin+\" min\":\"\u2014\",b.ok?b.cMin+\" min\":\"\u2014\",b.note||\"OK\"].map((v,i)=>(\n      <td key={i} style={{padding:\"5px 8px\",color:i===0?C.yellow:i===7?(parseFloat(v)>=2.0?C.green:C.red):i===8?C.yellow:i===9?C.teal:i===10?(v.includes(\"\u2605\")?C.green:v.includes(\"\u2717\")?C.red:C.dim):C.text,fontWeight:i===7?\"bold\":\"normal\",whiteSpace:\"nowrap\",fontFamily:M,fontSize:10}}>{v}</td>\n    ))}\n  </tr>\n);}\n\nfunction BatteryTab(){\n  const [v,setV]=useState(\"empty\");\n  return(<div>\n    <div style={{display:\"flex\",gap:6,marginBottom:14}}>\n      {[[\"empty\",\"Max Empty (2) 74HC74 hardware latch enables NX mode at POR \u2014 can only be cleared by physical tool action. (3) BCM2711 TrustZone secure monitor denies execute permission to any page flagged as originating from the SPI-SD device. (4) OTP fuse permanently marks the device class as non-executable.\"/>\n        <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"Filesystem navigation is fully permitted. The CM4 can ls, cat, stat, and read any file on the log SD. Only execute() system calls targeting files on this mount point are blocked \u2014 enforced simultaneously by the kernel noexec flag, SELinux policy, and the TrustZone secure monitor. An attacker who corrupts the CM4 OS cannot execute payloads stored on the log SD.\"/>\n        <Warn ch=\"The STM32 audit log fills at approximately 10,000 read-block hashes. When full, it rolls to a FIFO (oldest entries overwritten). For forensic analysis, retrieve the STM32 audit log via its dedicated UART debug port (JP4 on COMPHAT-1) before it rolls.\"/>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    {/* Zigbee/LoRa section */}\n    <SH t=\"Optional Zigbee + LoRa Radio Module\" c={C.accent}/>\n    <div style={{background:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.04)\",border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,padding:\"8px 12px\",marginBottom:14,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n      Both radio ICs are <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>optional population</span> \u2014 COMPHAT-1 PCB has footprints for both, populated on request. When not populated, their SPI buses are available as general-purpose GPIO. Flow\"/>\n    <Note c={C.teal} ch=\"For CC2652P7: install the desired protocol firmware image via Uniflash or oad_target over-the-air. Z-Stack (Zigbee), SimpleLink BLE5, and OpenThread images are available from TI. Only one image can be active at a time \u2014 flashing a new image takes ~30 seconds and requires the drone to be disarmed. The CM4 manages protocol switching via /dev/ttyUSB0 bootloader commands. For SX1276: the modulation mode (LoRa/FSK/OOK) is set by register writes over SPI \u2014 switching is instant, no reflash needed. Protocol stack (LoRaLib, custom Zigbee 915) is a CM4 userspace process that is started/stopped via systemctl.\"/>\n  </div>);\n}\n\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n//  REMAINING TABS \u2014 condensed from Rev D (Battery, Propulsion,\n//  Nav Lights, Antenna, Wiring, BOM, SBOM, Build Guide)\n// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n// Rev D performance constants (unchanged)\nconst BASE_70=582, T70=1100, I70=35, BAT_V=18.5, AV_A=2.0;\nconst CRUISE_A=I70*Math.pow(0.14,1.5)*2+14*0.88+AV_A;\nconst hovI=(auw)=>I70*Math.pow(auw/2/T70,1.5)*2+AV_A;\nfunction bRow(id,name,mass,cap,Cc,note,payload=0){\n  const auw=BASE_70+mass+payload,tw=(T70*2/auw).toFixed(2),ok=T70*2/auw>=2.0;\n  const hA=hovI(auw),hMin=(cap/1000*0.8/hA*60).toFixed(1),cMin=(cap/1000*0.8/CRUISE_A*60).toFixed(1);\n  return{id,name,mass,cap,Cc,note,auw,tw,ok,hA,hMin,cMin,maxA:cap/1000*Cc,payload};\n}\nconst BATS_E=[bRow(\"A\",\"5S 2800mAh 45C\",271,2800,45,\"\"),bRow(\"B\",\"5S 3500mAh 45C\",328,3500,45,\"\"),bRow(\"C\",\"5S 4000mAh 35C\",376,4000,35,\"\"),bRow(\"D\",\"5S 4500mAh 35C\",420,4500,35,\"\u2605 MAX ENDURANCE\"),bRow(\"E\",\"5S 5000mAh 30C\",465,5000,30,\"C-rating marginal\")];\nconst BATS_C=[bRow(\"A\",\"5S 1800mAh 75C\",190,1800,75,\"\",250),bRow(\"B\",\"5S 2200mAh 75C\",220,2200,75,\"\",250),bRow(\"C\",\"5S 2500mAh 60C\",248,2500,60,\"\",250),bRow(\"D\",\"5S 2800mAh 45C\",271,2800,45,\"\u2605 CARGO REC\",250),bRow(\"E\",\"5S 3000mAh 45C\",292,3000,45,\"T/W marginal\",250)];\nconst REC_E=BATS_E.find(b=>b.id===\"D\"),REC_C=BATS_C.find(b=>b.id===\"D\");\n\nfunction BatRow({b}){return(\n  <tr style={{background:b.note.includes(\"\u2605\")?\"rgba(74,222,128,0.06)\":b.id.charCodeAt(0)%2===0?\"rgba(0,229,255,0.02)\":\"transparent\"}}>\n    {[b.id,b.name,b.mass+\"g\",b.cap+\"\",b.Cc+\"C\",b.maxA.toFixed(0)+\"A\",b.auw+\"g\",b.tw,b.ok?b.hMin+\" min\":\"\u2014\",b.ok?b.cMin+\" min\":\"\u2014\",b.note||\"OK\"].map((v,i)=>(\n      <td key={i} style={{padding:\"5px    ))}\n  </tr>\n);}\n\nfunction BatteryTab(){\n  const [v,setV]=useState(\"empty\");\n  return(<div>\n    <div style={{display:\"flex\",gap:6,marginBottom:14}}>\n      {[[\"empty\",\"Max Empty {[[\"AUW\",v===\"empty\"?REC_E.auw:REC_C.auw,\"g\"],[\"T/W\",v===\"empty\"?REC_E.tw:REC_C.tw,\"\"],[\"Hover endurance\",v===\"empty\"?REC_E.hMin:REC_C.hMin,\"min\"],[\"Cruise        <td style={{padding:\"5px 8px\",color:C.dim,fontSize:9,lineHeight:1.5}}>{s.role}</td>\n        </tr>))}</tbody>\n      </table>\n    </div>\n    <Note c={C.lime} ch=\"CPLD Verilog write-blocker All CC BY 4.0 components (hull, nozzle) require attribution in derivative works.\"/>\n  </div>);\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 BUILD GUIDE (condensed \u2014 key Rev E additions highlighted) \u2500\nconst BUILD_ADDITIONS=[\n  {t:\"Install CPLD write-blocker (CM4-CARRIER-1)\",c:C.lime,d:\"The MachXO2-256ZE ships unprogrammed. Before board assembly: use a JTAG programmer (USB-Blaster or FT2232H breakout) to flash the write-blocker Verilog bitstream via ispDownload. Verify: insert a microSD, remove JP2 (WB disabled), verify OS installs normally. Replace JP2: verify OS mounts read-only and LED1 (green) lights.\"},\n  {t:\"Install diplexer and SMA connectors (CM4-CARRIER-1)\",c:C.purple,d:\"Solder MABD-011028 diplexer at U_DIP footprint. Solder two SMA edge-mount connectors at J_SMA1 (2.4GHz, orange-marked) and J_SMA2 (5GHz, purple-marked). Route 0.81mm semi-rigid coax from CM4 U.FL to diplexer input (\u226425mm). Verify 50\u03a9 impedance with a VNA before attaching antennas.\"},\n  {t:\"Install STM32 NX proxy and 74HC74 latch (COMPHAT-1)\",c:C.orange,d:\"Flash STM32F030F4P6 via SWD (ST-LINK V2) with nx_proxy.bin from the project repo. Burn write-protect OTP fuses via STM32CubeProgrammer (OB \u2192 Flash protection \u2192 set WRP to all sectors). After fuse blow, verify flash is read-protected. Install 74HC74 at U_NX footprint. At POR, NX_Q pin should read HIGH \u2014 verify with multimeter before first boot.\"},\n  {t:\"Populate CC2652P7 and SX1276 (optional \u2014 COMPHAT-1)\",c:C.accent,d:\"If Zigbee/LoRa is required: solder CC2652P7 at U_ZB footprint and SX1276 at U_LR footprint. Flash CC2652P7 with Z-Stack Zigbee image using Uniflash over JTAG. Test SX1276 with LoRaLib example sketch. Fit U.FL pigtails to SMA-ZB and SMA-LR board-edge connectors.\"},\n  {t:\"Verify multi-layer NX operation\",c:C.lime,d:\"Boot CM4. Check /proc/mounts: log-SD should show 'noexec,nodev,nosuid,ro'. Check SELinux context: ls -Z /mnt/log \u2014 should show 'system_u:object_r:log_storage_t:s0'. Attempt to execute a test binary copied to log-SD: should fail with EACCES. Read STM32 audit log via JP4 serial at 115200 baud: should show SHA-256 entries for boot reads. Verify TrustZone monitor active: cat /proc/device-tree/secure-chosen.\"},\n];\n\nfunction BuildGuideTab(){\n  const [open,setOpen]=useState(null);\n  const phases=[\n    {p:\"PHASE 1 \u00b7 PRINT PREP\",c:C.teal,steps:[\"Source PETG (300g), CF-PETG (80g), TPU 95A (40g). Dry all 6h at 65\u00b0C.\",\"Install hardened steel 0.4mm nozzle for CF-PETG. Calibrate E-steps and PA.\",\"Slice hull in 5 sections: nose cap (0\u201322mm), cockpit (22\u201390mm), mid-hull L+R halves (90\u2013230mm), aft-hull (230\u2013270mm), engine bell (270\u2013365mm). 8% gyroid, 2 perimeters, 0.20mm.\",\"Print TPU skid feet first. Then PETG hull sections overnight. Then CF-PETG nacelle pods and tilt brackets.\",\"Dry-fit all sections. Test nacelle bearing press-fit. Sand mating surfaces 220-grit.\"]},\n    {p:\"PHASE 2 \u00b7 CF SKELETON\",c:C.accent,steps:[\"Cut CF keel 6\u00d73mm \u00d7 385mm. Mark datums at 90, 130, 160, 200, 290, 365mm.\",\"Cut 2\u00d7 12mm CF tube \u00d7 300mm outrigger spars. Sand tenon 8mm dia \u00d7 25mm at one end.\",\"Epoxy mid-hull to keel. Cure 12h. Do not bond cockpit cap or engine bell \u2014 must be removable.\",\"Epoxy CF-PETG spar junction bracket at 130mm. Check 90\u00b0 alignment. Press-fit outrigger spars.\"]},\n    {p:\"PHASE 3 \u00b7 NACELLE ASSEMBLY\",c:C.orange,steps:[\"Press 8\u00d75\u00d72.5mm bearings into nacelle pivot sockets.\",\"Thread 8mm CF pivot rod through spar bracket \u2192 bearing \u2192 nacelle. Secure with M3 set screw + Loctite 243.\",\"Install 70mm EDF into nacelle pod. Route motor leads through hollow CF spar.\",\"Mount MG90S tilt servo to CF-PETG bracket. Connect servo horn to nacelle push-rod at 18mm radius.\",\"Install WS2812C-2020 LED into nacelle tip cap PETG lens recess (port=red, stbd=green). Epoxy, seal.\"]},\n    {p:\"PHASE 4 \u00b7 ELECTRONICS\",c:C.purple,steps:[\"Mount Pico 2 + TRIHAT-1 stack at 60\u201390mm on cockpit CF bulkhead plate (55\u00d738mm 2mm CF, cut to fit).\",\"Mount CM4 stack (CM4 + CM4-CARRIER-1 Rev E + COMPHAT-1 Rev E) at 95\u2013160mm avionics bay floor. Install OS \u03bcSD in carrier slot, log \u03bcSD in COMPHAT slot.\",\"Connect GPS U.FL coax to TRIHAT-1, route to cockpit roof GPS patch antenna.\",\"Install 3\u00d7 ESCs on keel underside with double-sided foam + M2.5 screw. Test all 6 light states.\",\"First power-on bench test: verify no shorts, all regulators in range, all boards booting, MAVLink heartbeat in QGC.\"]},\n    {p:\"PHASE 7 \u00b7 SOFTWARE & CALIBRATION\",c:C.pink,steps:[\"Flash Pico 2 firmware (USB drag-drop). Configure mavlink-router.\",\"Configure SELinux: label log-SD mount point as log_storage_t. Mount with noexec,nodev,nosuid,ro. Here's the design rationale for each new element:\n\n**CC BY 4.0 License (Tab 2):** The License tab includes the full attribution template, a component license map (Peter Farell hull / BamJr nozzle / this design all CC BY 4.0), what's covered vs. what falls under separate terms (GPL-3 SiK firmware, TI TSPA for CC2652P7 Z-Stack, etc.), and a forensic evidence integrity notice clarifying the write-blocker is not NIST-certified hardware.\n\n**Forensic Write-Blocker \u2014 OS SD (CM4 Carrier tab):** A Lattice MachXO2-256ZE CPLD sits inline on all 6 SDIO0 signals between the CM4 and the microSD slot. It's fully combinatorial \u2014 no buffering, just opcode pattern matching on the CMD line. When it detects CMD24/25/28/29/32\u201338/42, it injects a valid R1 response with bit 19 (WP_VIOLATION) set before the card ever sees the command. JP2 is a recessed 2-pin jumper \u2014 install for forensic mode, remove only to (re)flash the OS. Two indicator LEDs: green = blocking active, red flashes = write attempt detected and blocked. The Verilog source will be MIT licensed in the project repo.\n\n**Hardware NX \u2014 Log SD (COMPHAT-1 tab):** This is genuinely multi-layer: (1) STM32F030 SPI proxy physically intercepts all SPI traffic and only passes CMD17/18 read commands \u2014 write/erase commands are absorbed. Its own flash has OTP write-protect fuses burned at manufacture so the NX firmware is immutable. (2) A 74HC74 D flip-flop latches HIGH at power-on \u2014 hardware NX mode is *on by default* and can only be cleared by a tool-requiring physical jumper (JP3, recessed). (3) BCM2711 TrustZone secure monitor enforces noexec on pages whose origin is flagged as SPI-SD. (4) SELinux type `log_storage_t` + kernel `noexec` mount flag as software backstop. The STM32 maintains a rolling SHA-256 audit log readable via JP4 serial.\n\n**Dual-Band WiFi SMA:** The MABD-011028 diplexer splits the CM4's single U.FL antenna port into a 2.4GHz path (SMA-1, orange) and a 5GHz path (SMA-2, purple) with 0.3\u20130.4dB insertion loss and 45dB isolation. The BCM43455 auto-disables the internal PCB trace antenna when it detects a load on the U.FL connector \u2014 no software configuration needed. The carrier board grows to 65\u00d752mm (+12mm width).\n\n**Zigbee + LoRa (COMPHAT-1 tab, protocol inspector):** CC2652P7 handles all 2.4GHz protocols (Zigbee, BLE 5.0, Thread/Matter, LoRa 2.4GHz) \u2014 one firmware image active at a time, switched via Uniflash reflash or OAD. SX1276 handles sub-GHz (LoRa 915MHz primary; FSK mode enables a software Zigbee 915MHz stack on the CM4). Both ICs are optional-population footprints on COMPHAT-1 \u2014 the PCB is designed for both, but you only solder what you need. The protocol inspector in the tab lets you click through all 5 protocol modes to see their specs.",

C = {\n  bg:\"#060a0e\", border:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.11)\", accent:\"#00e5ff\",\n  orange:\"#ff6b35\", yellow:\"#ffe600\", purple:\"#c084fc\", green:\"#4ade80\",\n  pink:\"#f472b6\", teal:\"#2dd4bf\", red:\"#f87171\", lime:\"#a3e635\",\n  gold:\"#fbbf24\", dim:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.40)\", dimmer:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.22)\",\n  text:\"rgba(255,255,255,0.82)\",\n};\nconst M = \"'Courier New','Lucida Console',monospace\";\n\n// \u2500\u2500 primitives \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nconst Warn=({ch})=>(<div style={{marginTop:8,color:C.yellow,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,lineHeight:1.8,padding:\"7px 12px\",borderLeft:`2px solid ${C.yellow}`,background:\"rgba(255,230,0,0.05)\",borderRadius:3}}>\u26a0 {ch}</div>);\nfunction TH({cols}){return(<thead><tr>{cols.map(h=>(<th key={h} style={{padding:\"6px 9px\",borderBottom:`1px solid ${C.border}`,color:C.accent,textAlign:\"left\",fontWeight:\"normal\",fontSize:10,letterSpacing:\"0.06em\",whiteSpace:\"nowrap\",opacity:.85}}>{h}</th>))}</tr></thead>);}\nfunction Grid(){return(<svg style={{position:\"fixed\",inset:0,width:\"100%\",height:\"100%\",pointerEvents:\"none\",zIndex:0}}><defs><pattern id=\"sg\" width=\"20\" height=\"20\" patternUnits=\"userSpaceOnUse\"><path d=\"M20 0L0 0 0 20\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.03)\" strokeWidth={0.5}/></pattern><pattern id=\"lg\" width=\"100\" height=\"100\" patternUnits=\"userSpaceOnUse\"><rect width=\"100\" height=\"100\" fill=\"url(#sg)\"/><path d=\"M100 0L0 0 0 100\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.06)\" strokeWidth={1}/></pattern></defs><rect width=\"100%\" height=\"100%\" fill=\"url(#lg)\"/></svg>);}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//  GEAR MECHANISM CONSTANTS\n//  Nacelle pivot:   90\u00b0 sweep (0\u00b0 cruise \u2192 90\u00b0 hover)\n//  Sector gear:     R_sec = 22mm  (fixed to tilt bracket)\n//  Drive pinion:    R_pin =  6mm  (on nacelle outer shell)\n//  Ratio:           22/6 \u2248 3.67:1  \u2192  90\u00b0 nacelle = 330\u00b0 pinion\n//  Bevel pair:      1:1 (90\u00b0 axis redirect, pinion\u2192longitudinal shaft)\n//  Longitudinal shaft \u2192 Crown pinion: R_cr = 6mm\n//  Nozzle ring rack: R_rack = 28mm  (radius of rack on inner ring)\n//  Ring angle:      330 \u00d7 (6/28) \u2248 70.7\u00b0  (62\u201375\u00b0 target range)\n//  Result:          ~71\u00b0 ring rotation for full 90\u00b0 nacelle sweep  \u2714\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\nconst GEAR = {\n  nacelle_sweep: 90,       // deg\n  R_sector: 22,            // mm - sector gear radius (on fixed bracket)\n  R_pinion: 6,             // mm - drive pinion radius (on nacelle body)\n  bevel_ratio: 1.0,        // 1:1 bevel pair\n  R_crown: 6,              // mm - crown pinion radius (longitudinal axis)\n  R_rack: 28,              // mm - nozzle inner ring effective rack radius\n};\nGEAR.step1 = GEAR.nacelle_sweep * GEAR.R_sector / GEAR.R_pinion; // 330\u00b0\nGEAR.step2 = GEAR.step1 * GEAR.bevel_ratio;                       // 330\u00b0\nGEAR.ring_angle = GEAR.step2 * GEAR.R_crown / GEAR.R_rack;        // 70.7\u00b0\nGEAR.nozzle_open_dia  = 42; // mm at hover (ring angle = max)\nGEAR.nozzle_close_dia = 36; // mm at cruise (ring angle = 0)\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//  TAB 1: MECHANISM OVERVIEW\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// Side-view diagram: nacelle at two positions with gear indicators\nfunction PositionDiagram({angle}){\n  // angle: 0 = cruise (horizontal), 90 = hover (vertical)\n  const VW=360, VH=340, PX=180, PY=170; // pivot point\n  const NAC_L=90, NAC_W=18;  // nacelle length/half-width in px\n  const rad = angle * Math.PI / 180;\n  // nacelle body direction unit vector (aft = cos/sin of angle from horizontal)\n  const dx = Math.cos(rad), dy = -Math.sin(rad);\n  // nacelle endpoints\n  const fwdX = PX - dx*NAC_L*0.35, fwdY = PY - dy*NAC_L*0.35; // intake end\n  const aftX = PX + dx*NAC_L*0.65, aftY = PY + dy*NAC_L*0.65; // nozzle end\n  // perpendicular for nacelle width\n  const px2 = -dy * NAC_W, py2 = dx * NAC_W;\n  // nacelle corners\n  const c1x=fwdX+px2, c1y=fwdY+py2, c2x=fwdX-px2, c2y=fwdY-py2;\n  const c3x=aftX-px2, c3y=aftY-py2, c4x=aftX+px2, c4y=aftY+py2;\n  // gear state\n  const isHover = angle === 90;\n  const nozzleDia = isHover ? GEAR.nozzle_open_dia : GEAR.nozzle_close_dia;\n  const nozzleLabel = isHover ? \"OPEN\" : \"CLOSED\";\n  const nozzleColor = isHover ? C.green : C.orange;\n  // nozzle ring at aft\n  const nozzR = (nozzleDia/2) * 1.4; // scaled\n  // sector gear (fixed to bracket, at pivot)\n  const secR = GEAR.R_sector * 1.5;\n  const arcStart = -10 * Math.PI / 180;\n  const arcEnd   = (90+10) * Math.PI / 180;\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:380,display:\"block\"}}>\n      {/* Wing spar */}\n      <rect x={PX-6} y={PY-120} width={12} height={240} rx={3} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.08)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.2}/>\n      {/* Tilt bracket */}\n      <circle cx={PX} cy={PY} r={18} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.06)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      <circle cx={PX} cy={PY} r={5} fill={C.accent} opacity={0.7}/>\n      <text x={PX} y={PY-22} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.accent} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>PIVOT</text>\n\n      {/* Sector gear arc (FIXED, on bracket, sweeps 0-90\u00b0) */}\n      {/* In SVG: 0\u00b0 = right, CCW positive. We want 0\u00b0 to 90\u00b0 CCW from \"right\" */}\n      {/* Sector drawn as filled path */}\n      <path d={`M${PX},${PY} L${PX+secR},${PY} A${secR},${secR} 0 0,0 ${PX},${PY-secR} Z`}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,230,0,0.07)\" stroke={C.yellow} strokeWidth={1.2} strokeDasharray=\"4 2\" opacity={0.7}/>\n      {/* Sector gear teeth hint */}\n      {Array.from({length:7},(_,i)=>{\n        const a = i * 15 * Math.PI/180;\n        const r1=secR-4, r2=secR+4;\n        return <line key={i} x1={PX+r1*Math.cos(-a)} y1={PY+r1*Math.sin(-a)}\n          x2={PX+r2*Math.cos(-a)} y2={PY+r2*Math.sin(-a)}\n          stroke={C.yellow} strokeWidth={2} opacity={0.5}/>;\n      })}\n      <text x={PX+secR+10} y={PY-secR/2} fill={C.yellow} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>SECTOR</text>\n      <text x={PX+secR+10} y={PY-secR/2+10} fill={`${C.yellow}60`} fontSize={6} fontFamily={M}>R={GEAR.R_sector}mm</text>\n      <text x={PX+secR+10} y={PY-secR/2+20} fill={`${C.yellow}60`} fontSize={6} fontFamily={M}>FIXED to bracket</text>\n\n      {/* Nacelle body */}\n      <polygon points={`${c1x},${c1y} ${c2x},${c2y} ${c3x},${c3y} ${c4x},${c4y}`}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,107,53,0.1)\" stroke={C.orange} strokeWidth={1.8}/>\n      {/* EDF fan disk at intake */}\n      <circle cx={fwdX} cy={fwdY} r={NAC_W*0.85}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,107,53,0.12)\" stroke={C.orange} strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray=\"2 2\"/>\n      {/* Nozzle ring at aft */}\n      <circle cx={aftX} cy={aftY} r={nozzR}\n        fill={`${nozzleColor}18`} stroke={nozzleColor} strokeWidth={2}/>\n      <circle cx={aftX} cy={aftY} r={nozzR*0.55}\n        fill={`${nozzleColor}20`} stroke={nozzleColor} strokeWidth={1}/>\n      <text x={aftX} y={aftY+3} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={nozzleColor} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">{nozzleLabel}</text>\n      <text x={aftX} y={aftY+14} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${nozzleColor}80`} fontSize={6} fontFamily={M}>{nozzleDia}mm exit</text>\n\n      {/* Drive pinion on nacelle body (at pivot perimeter) */}\n      <circle cx={PX+GEAR.R_pinion*1.5*Math.cos(rad)} cy={PY-GEAR.R_pinion*1.5*Math.sin(rad)}\n        r={GEAR.R_pinion*1.5} fill=\"rgba(45,212,191,0.15)\" stroke={C.teal} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      <text x={PX+(GEAR.R_pinion*1.5+14)*Math.cos(rad+0.6)}\n            y={PY-(GEAR.R_pinion*1.5+14)*Math.sin(rad+0.6)}\n        fill={C.teal} fontSize={6} fontFamily={M}>PINION R={GEAR.R_pinion}</text>\n\n      {/* Pushrod line from pinion to nozzle ring */}\n      <line x1={PX+GEAR.R_pinion*1.5*Math.cos(rad)} y1={PY-GEAR.R_pinion*1.5*Math.sin(rad)}\n        x2={aftX} y2={aftY}\n        stroke={C.teal} strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray=\"3 3\" opacity={0.6}/>\n\n      {/* Angle label */}\n      <text x={PX-55} y={PY+5} fill={C.orange} fontSize={12} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">{angle}\u00b0</text>\n      <text x={PX-55} y={PY+18} fill={`${C.orange}70`} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M}>{isHover?\"HOVER\":\"CRUISE\"}</text>\n\n      {/* Ring rotation indicator */}\n      {[0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7].map(i=>{\n        const flap_a = isHover\n          ? (i * 45 * Math.PI/180)\n          : (i * 45 * Math.PI/180 + 0.6);\n        const r1=nozzR-3, r2=nozzR+5;\n        return <line key={i}\n          x1={aftX+r1*Math.cos(flap_a)} y1={aftY+r1*Math.sin(flap_a)}\n          x2={aftX+r2*Math.cos(flap_a)} y2={aftY+r2*Math.sin(flap_a)}\n          stroke={nozzleColor} strokeWidth={2.5} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.8}/>;\n      })}\n\n      <text x={VW/2} y={14} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.25)\"\n        fontSize={9} fontFamily={M} letterSpacing=\"0.1em\">\n        {isHover?\"HOVER \u2014 NACELLE 90\u00b0 \u00b7 NOZZLE OPEN\":\"CRUISE \u2014 NACELLE 0\u00b0 \u00b7 NOZZLE CLOSED\"}\n      </text>\n    </svg>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction GearSchematicFull(){\n  const VW=700, VH=420;\n  // Layout: tilt bracket left, nacelle body centre, nozzle ring right\n  const BX=100, BY=210; // bracket/pivot\n  const NX=340, NY=210; // nacelle longitudinal axis centre (bevel gear point)\n  const ZX=580, ZY=210; // nozzle ring centre\n\n  function gearCircle(cx,cy,r,n,color,fill=\"\"){\n    const pts=[];\n    for(let i=0;i<n;i++){\n      const a=i*2*Math.PI/n;\n      const ra=r-3, rb=r+3;\n      const a1=a-Math.PI/n*0.4, a2=a+Math.PI/n*0.4;\n      pts.push(`M${cx+ra*Math.cos(a1)},${cy+ra*Math.sin(a1)}`);\n      pts.push(`A${ra},${ra} 0 0,1 ${cx+ra*Math.cos(a2)},${cy+ra*Math.sin(a2)}`);\n      pts.push(`L${cx+rb*Math.cos(a2)},${cy+rb*Math.sin(a2)}`);\n      pts.push(`A${rb},${rb} 0 0,0 ${cx+rb*Math.cos(a1)},${cy+rb*Math.sin(a1)}`);\n      pts.push(\"Z\");\n    }\n    return(<>\n      <circle cx={cx} cy={cy} r={r} fill={fill||`${color}18`} stroke={color} strokeWidth={1}/>\n      <path d={pts.join(\" \")} fill={color} opacity={0.55}/>\n      <circle cx={cx} cy={cy} r={3} fill={color} opacity={0.8}/>\n    </>);\n  }\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n      {/* Wing spar */}\n      <rect x={BX-8} y={30} width={16} height={380} rx={4} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.06)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.2}/>\n      <text x={BX} y={18} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.accent} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M}>WING SPAR</text>\n\n      {/* Tilt bracket */}\n      <rect x={BX-28} y={BY-55} width={56} height={110} rx={6} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.05)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1.5} strokeDasharray=\"6 3\"/>\n      <text x={BX} y={BY+75} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.accent} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M}>TILT BRACKET</text>\n      <text x={BX} y={BY+87} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.accent}55`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>(fixed to spar)</text>\n\n      {/* SECTOR GEAR on bracket */}\n      {/* Sector arc 90\u00b0 (0\u00b0 right \u2192 90\u00b0 up in SVG = -90\u00b0) */}\n      <path d={`M${BX},${BY} L${BX+33},${BY} A33,33 0 0,0 ${BX},${BY-33} Z`}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,230,0,0.1)\" stroke={C.yellow} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      {Array.from({length:5},(_,i)=>{\n        const a=-i*22.5*Math.PI/180;\n        return <line key={i} x1={BX+28*Math.cos(a)} y1={BY+28*Math.sin(a)}\n          x2={BX+36*Math.cos(a)} y2={BY+36*Math.sin(a)}\n          stroke={C.yellow} strokeWidth={2.5} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.7}/>;\n      })}\n      <circle cx={BX} cy={BY} r={5} fill={C.yellow} opacity={0.8}/>\n      <text x={BX+40} y={BY-20} fill={C.yellow} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">SECTOR GEAR</text>\n      <text x={BX+40} y={BY-8} fill={`${C.yellow}70`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>R={GEAR.R_sector}mm \u00b7 90\u00b0 arc</text>\n      <text x={BX+40} y={BY+4} fill={`${C.yellow}50`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>FIXED to tilt bracket</text>\n\n      {/* Nacelle body rectangle */}\n      <rect x={BX+30} y={BY-22} width={NX-BX+40} height={44} rx={5}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,107,53,0.06)\" stroke={C.orange} strokeWidth={1.8}/>\n      <text x={(BX+30+NX+40)/2} y={BY+3} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.orange}50`} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M}>NACELLE BODY (rotates with tilt)</text>\n\n      {/* Drive pinion on nacelle, meshes with sector */}\n      {gearCircle(BX+22, BY-22, GEAR.R_pinion*3.2, 12, C.teal)}\n      <text x={BX+22} y={BY-40} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.teal} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">PINION A</text>\n      <text x={BX+22} y={BY-50} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.teal}70`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>R={GEAR.R_pinion}mm \u00b7 12T</text>\n      <text x={BX+22} y={BY-60} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.teal}50`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>on nacelle outer wall</text>\n\n      {/* Shaft from pinion A to bevel gear (runs through nacelle wall transversely) */}\n      <line x1={BX+22} y1={BY-22} x2={BX+22} y2={BY+22}\n        stroke={C.teal} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.5}/>\n      <text x={BX+35} y={BY+16} fill={`${C.teal}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>shaft</text>\n\n      {/* Bevel gear 1 (transverse axis \u2192 longitudinal axis) on nacelle wall */}\n      {gearCircle(BX+80, BY, 14, 14, C.lime)}\n      <text x={BX+80} y={BY+25} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.lime} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">BEVEL A</text>\n      <text x={BX+80} y={BY+36} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.lime}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>14T \u00b7 14mm</text>\n      <text x={BX+80} y={BY-20} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.lime}45`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>transverse axis</text>\n\n      {/* Bevel gear 2 (longitudinal axis) */}\n      {gearCircle(BX+114, BY, 14, 14, C.lime)}\n      <text x={BX+114} y={BY+25} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.lime} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">BEVEL B</text>\n      <text x={BX+114} y={BY+36} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.lime}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>14T \u00b7 14mm</text>\n      <text x={BX+114} y={BY-20} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.lime}45`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>long. axis</text>\n\n      {/* Bevel engagement indicator */}\n      <line x1={BX+80} y1={BY} x2={BX+114} y2={BY} stroke={C.lime} strokeWidth={1.5} strokeDasharray=\"3 2\" opacity={0.5}/>\n      <text x={(BX+80+BX+114)/2} y={BY-30} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.lime} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>1:1 bevel pair</text>\n      <text x={(BX+80+BX+114)/2} y={BY-20} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.lime}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>90\u00b0 axis redirect</text>\n\n      {/* Longitudinal shaft to crown pinion */}\n      <line x1={BX+114} y1={BY} x2={NX-20} y2={BY}\n        stroke={C.lime} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.5}/>\n      <text x={(BX+114+NX-20)/2} y={BY-10} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.lime}50`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>long. shaft (3mm CF rod)</text>\n\n      {/* Crown pinion at longitudinal shaft end */}\n      {gearCircle(NX-6, BY, GEAR.R_crown*3.2, 10, C.purple)}\n      <text x={NX-6} y={BY-28} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.purple} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">CROWN PIN.</text>\n      <text x={NX-6} y={BY-38} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.purple}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>R={GEAR.R_crown}mm \u00b7 10T</text>\n\n      {/* Nozzle inner ring */}\n      <circle cx={ZX} cy={ZY} r={GEAR.R_rack*2.6} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.05)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={2}/>\n      <circle cx={ZX} cy={ZY} r={20} fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.1)\" stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray=\"3 2\"/>\n      <text x={ZX} y={ZY+3} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.accent} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">NOZZLE RING</text>\n      <text x={ZX} y={ZY+15} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.accent}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>R_rack={GEAR.R_rack}mm</text>\n\n      {/* Rack teeth on inner ring (partial arc) */}\n      {Array.from({length:10},(_,i)=>{\n        const a = (i-5) * 7 * Math.PI/180;\n        const r1=GEAR.R_rack*2.6-5, r2=GEAR.R_rack*2.6+5;\n        return <line key={i}\n          x1={ZX+r1*Math.cos(Math.PI+a)} y1={ZY+r1*Math.sin(Math.PI+a)}\n          x2={ZX+r2*Math.cos(Math.PI+a)} y2={ZY+r2*Math.sin(Math.PI+a)}\n          stroke={C.purple} strokeWidth={2.5} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.6}/>;\n      })}\n      <text x={ZX-82} y={ZY+15} fill={C.purple} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>rack teeth on ring ID</text>\n\n      {/* Shaft from crown pinion to ring */}\n      <line x1={NX-6} y1={BY} x2={ZX-GEAR.R_rack*2.6} y2={ZY}\n        stroke={C.purple} strokeWidth={2.5} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.5}/>\n\n      {/* Flaps on nozzle ring */}\n      {[0,45,90,135,180,225,270,315].map(a=>{\n        const ar=a*Math.PI/180, r1=GEAR.R_rack*2.6, r2=r1+16;\n        return <line key={a}\n          x1={ZX+r1*Math.cos(ar)} y1={ZY+r1*Math.sin(ar)}\n          x2={ZX+r2*Math.cos(ar)} y2={ZY+r2*Math.sin(ar)}\n          stroke={C.green} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.7}/>;\n      })}\n      <text x={ZX} y={ZY-GEAR.R_rack*2.6-12}      {[\n        [\"Nacelle sweep\",\"90\u00b0\",C.orange],\n        [\"\u00d7 R_sec/R_pin\",\"\u00d7\"+GEAR.R_sector+\"/\"+GEAR.R_pinion+\" = \u00d73.67\",C.yellow],\n        [\"= Pinion A \u2192 ring at 0\u00b0 (nozzle closed 36mm) \u00b7 Nacelle 90\u00b0 (hover) \u2192 ring at 70.7\u00b0 (nozzle open 42mm)\n      </text>\n      <text x={VW/2} y={16} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill=\"rgba(0,229,255,0.25)\"\n        fontSize={9} fontFamily={M} letterSpacing=\"0.1em\">NACELLE NOZZLE GEAR COUPLING \u2014 SCHEMATIC VIEW</text>\n    </svg>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction OverviewTab(){\n  const [nacPos,setNacPos]=useState(90);\n  return(<div>\n    <div style={{background:\"rgba(255,107,53,0.05)\",border:`1px solid rgba(255,107,53,0.2)`,borderRadius:4,padding:\"10px 14px\",marginBottom:18,fontFamily:M,fontSize:11,color:C.dim,lineHeight:1.8}}>\n      <span style={{color:C.orange,fontWeight:\"bold\"}}>Remix:</span> BamJr thing:2991269 (CC BY 4.0) \u00b7\n      scaled to <span style={{color:C.yellow}}>70mm ID</span> for nacelle EDFs \u00b7\n      sector gear coupling to nacelle pivot \u2014 <span style={{color:C.green}}>no separate servo</span> \u00b7\n      passive mechanical linkage \u00b7 nozzle opens automatically as nacelle tilts to hover.\n    </div>\n\n    <div style={{display:\"grid\",gridTemplateColumns:\"1fr 1fr\",gap:20,marginBottom:20}}>\n      <div>\n        <div style={{color:C.dimmer,fontFamily:M,fontSize:10,letterSpacing:\"0.08em\",marginBottom:8}}>\n          INTERACTIVE \u2014 click position to preview\n        </div>\n        <div style={{display:\"flex\",gap:8,marginBottom:12}}>\n          {[[0,\"CRUISE 0\u00b0\"],[45,\"TRANSITION 45\u00b0\"],[90,\"HOVER 90\u00b0\"]].map(([a,l])=>(\n            <button key={a} onClick={()=>setNacPos(a)} style={{\n              background:nacPos===a?\"rgba(255,107,53,0.15)\":\"transparent\",\n              border:`1px solid ${nacPos===a?C.orange:\"rgba(255,107,53,0.2)\"}`,\n              color:nacPos===a?C.orange:C.dimmer,\n              padding:\"4px 10px\",fontFamily:M,fontSize:9,cursor:\"pointer\",borderRadius:2}}>{l}</button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n        <div style={{border:`1px solid ${C.border}`,borderRadius:4,background:\"rgba(0,229,255,0.01)\",padding:8}}>\n          <PositionDiagram angle={nacPos}/>\n        </div>\n        {nacPos===45&&(\n          <Note c={C.teal} ch={`At 45\u00b0: nozzle ring at ~${(GEAR.ring_angle*0.5).toFixed(0)}\u00b0 rotation \u2192 exit dia ~${(36+(42-36)*0.5).toFixed(0)}mm. Smooth linear interpolation between cruise and hover \u2014 no stepwise actuator.`}/>\n        )}\n      </div>\n      <div>\n        <SH t=\"Operating Logic\" mt={0}/>\n        <KV k=\"Nacelle at 0\u00b0 (cruise)\" v=\"Nozzle CLOSED \u00b7 36mm exit dia\" vc={C.orange}/>\n        <KV k=\"Nacelle at 90\u00b0 (hover)\" v=\"Nozzle OPEN \u00b7 42mm exit dia\" vc={C.green}/>\n        <KV k=\"Nacelle at 45\u00b0 (transition)\" v=\"Nozzle ~50% \u00b7 39mm exit dia\" vc={C.teal}/>\n        <KV k=\"Actuation\" v=\"Passive mechanical \u2014 no servo, no power\" vc={C.green}/>\n        <KV k=\"Coupling\" v=\"Sector gear (bracket) + bevel pair + crown pinion + ring rack\"/>\n        <KV k=\"Total gear ratio chain\" v={`${GEAR.nacelle_sweep}\u00b0 \u2192 ${GEAR.ring_angle.toFixed(1)}\u00b0 ring`} vc={C.yellow}/>\n        <KV k=\"Hover thrust gain\" v=\"~8\u201312% vs fixed nozzle (open 115% area)\" vc={C.green}/>\n        <KV k=\"Cruise Isp gain\" v=\"~15\u201318% jet velocity (closed 82% area)\" vc={C.teal}/>\n        <KV k=\"Fail-safe\" v=\"If gear jams: nozzle stays at last position. EDF still flies normally \u2014 nozzle is not critical for flight.\"/>\n        <KV k=\"Lubrication\" v=\"Sewing machine oil on all gear interfaces \u00b7 re-apply every 20 flights\"/>\n        <SH t=\"Why this direction?\"/>\n        <Note c={C.orange} ch=\"Cruise (0\u00b0): the 70mm fans act as forward thrust jets. Closing the nozzle reduces exit area, increasing jet velocity and propulsive efficiency \u2014 exactly like a jet engine's converging nozzle at cruise. Hover (90\u00b0): the fans provide lift. Opening the nozzle reduces backpressure, allowing higher mass flow through the fan and increasing total thrust. This is the classic trade-off between thrust (open) and specific impulse (closed).\"/>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <SH t=\"Full Gear Coupling Schematic\"/>\n    <div style={{border:`1px solid GearDetailDiagram(){\n  const VW=640, VH=360;\n  // Detailed cross-section through nacelle side wall\n  const WX=80, WY=180, WW=14; // wall\n  const pinionCX=WX+WW+GEAR.R_pinion*5; // pinion centre\n  const bevelCX=pinionCX+60; // bevel 1\n  const bevel2X=bevelCX+32; // bevel 2 (longitudinal)\n\n  function teeth(cx,cy,r,n,angle,color){\n    return Array.from({length:n},(_,i)=>{\n      const a = i*2*Math.PI/n + angle;\n      const r1=r-4, r2=r+4;\n      return <line key={i}\n        x1={cx+r1*Math.cos(a)} y1={cy+r1*Math.sin(a)}\n        x2={cx+r2*Math.cos(a)} y2={cy+r2*Math.sin(a)}\n        stroke={color} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.7}/>;\n    });\n  }\n\n  return(\n    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${VW} ${VH}`} width=\"100%\" style={{maxWidth:\"100%\",display:\"block\"}}>\n      {/* Nacelle outer wall (cross section) */}\n      <rect x={WX} y={WY-70} width={WW} height={140} rx={2}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,107,53,0.1)\" stroke={C.orange} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      <text x={WX+WW/2} y={WY-78} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.orange} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>WALL</text>\n      <text x={WX+WW/2} y={WY-67} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.orange}60`} fontSize={6} fontFamily={M}>2mm CF-PETG</text>\n\n      {/* Sector gear (left of wall, fixed to bracket) */}\n      <path d={`M${WX-8},${WY} L${WX-8-35},${WY} A35,35 0 0,0 ${WX-8},${WY-35} Z`}\n        fill=\"rgba(255,230,0,0.1)\" stroke={C.yellow} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      {Array.from({length:5},(_,i)=>{\n        const a=-i*22*Math.PI/180;\n        return <line key={i} x1={WX-8+29*Math.cos(a)} y1={WY+29*Math.sin(a)}\n          x2={WX-8+38*Math.cos(a)} y2={WY+38*Math.sin(a)}\n          stroke={C.yellow} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.7}/>;\n      })}\n      <text x={WX-50} y={WY-42} fill={C.yellow} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">SECTOR</text>\n      <text x={WX-50} y={WY-30} fill={`${C.yellow}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>22mm R</text>\n      <text x={WX-50} y={WY-20} fill={`${C.yellow}50`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>M0.5 \u00b7 22\u00b0 PA</text>\n      <text x={WX-50} y={WY+2} fill={`${C.yellow}40`} fontSize={6} fontFamily={M}>FIXED to bracket</text>\n\n      {/* Drive pinion A (on nacelle outer face, transverse shaft) */}\n      <circle cx={pinionCX} cy={WY} r={GEAR.R_pinion*5}\n        fill=\"rgba(45,212,191,0.12)\" stroke={C.teal} strokeWidth={1.5}/>\n      {teeth(pinionCX,WY,GEAR.R_pinion*5,12,0,C.teal)}\n      <line x1={pinionCX} y1={WY-GEAR.R_pinion*5-10} x2={pinionCX} y2={WY+GEAR.R_pinion*5+10}\n        stroke={C.teal} strokeWidth={2.5} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.6}/>\n      <text x={pinionCX} y={WY-GEAR.R_pinion*5-18} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={C.teal} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">PINION A</text>\n      <text x={pinionCX} y={WY-GEAR.R_pinion*5-7} textAnchor=\"middle\" fill={`${C.teal}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>6mm R \u00b7 12T \u00b7 M0.5</text>\n      <text x={pinionCX+GEAR.R_pinion*5+8} y={WY+3} fill={`${C.teal}50`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>3mm \u00f8 shaft</text>\n      <text x={pinionCX+GEAR.R_pinion*5+8} y={WY+14} fill={`${C.teal}40`} stroke=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.15)\" strokeWidth={1} strokeDasharray=\"2 2\"/>\n      <text x={(WX-8+pinionCX-GEAR.R_pinion*5)/2} y={WY+12} textAnchor=\"middle\"\n        fill=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.3)\" to crown pinion */}\n      <line x1={bevel2X+22} y1={WY} x2={520} y2={WY}\n        stroke={C.purple} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.5}/>\n      <text x={(bevel2X+22+520)/2} textAnchor=\"middle\"\n        fill={`${C.purple}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>{GEAR.R_crown}mm R \u00b7 10T</text>\n\n      {/* Nozzle inner ring (partially visible) */}\n      {/* Ring arc {Array.from({length:8},(_,i)=>{\n        const a=(-90+i*25)*Math.PI/180;\n        return <line key={i} x1={580+82*Math.cos(a)} y1={WY+82*Math.sin(a)}\n          x2={580+92*Math.cos(a)} y2={WY+92*Math.sin(a)}\n          stroke={C.accent} strokeWidth={3} strokeLinecap=\"round\" opacity={0.6}/>;\n      })}\n      <text x={600} y={WY} fill={C.accent} fontSize={8} fontFamily={M} fontWeight=\"bold\">NOZZLE</text>\n      <text x={600} y={WY+12} fill={`${C.accent}60`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>INNER RING</text>\n      <text x={600} y={WY+24} fill={`${C.accent}50`} fontSize={7} fontFamily={M}>R={GEAR.R_rack}mm</text>\n\n      {/* Mesh point */}\n      <circle cx={580} cy={WY} r={5} fill={C.purple} opacity={0.6}/>\n\n      {/* Annotations */}\n      <text x={VW/2} y={16} );\n}\n\nfunction GearDesignTab(){\n  return(<div>\n    <SH t=\"Gear Train Detail\" mt={0}/>\n    <div style={{border:`1px solid           {g:\"Sector gear\",m:\"M0.5\",t:\"~86T (90\u00b0 arc)\",r:\"22mm\",mat:\"PETG\",n:\"Fixed to tilt bracket \u00b7 90\u00b0 arc only\"},\n              {g:\"Pinion A\",m:\"M0.5\",t:\"12T\",r:\"6mm\",mat:\"CF-PETG\",n:\"On nacelle wall \u00b7 3mm shaft\"},\n              {g:\"Bevel A\",m:\"M0.5\",t:\"14T\",r:\"14mm\",mat:\"CF-PETG\",n:\"Transverse axis \u00b7 20\u00b0 bevel angle\"},\n              {g:\"Bevel B\",m:\"M0.5\",t:\"14T\",r:\"14mm\",mat:\"CF-PETG\",n:\"Longitudinal axis \u00b7 90\u00b0 axis change\"},\n              {g:\"Crown pinion\",m:\"M0.5\",t:\"10T\",r:\"6mm\",mat:\"CF-PETG\",n:\"Drives rack on nozzle ring\"},\n              {g:\"Nozzle ring rack\",m:\"M0.5\",t:\"partial arc\",r:\"28mm (eff.)\",mat:\"PETG\",n:\"On </table>\n        </div>\n\n        <SH t=\"Gear Ratio Cascade\"/>\n        <KV k=\"Step 1: Sector \u2192 Pinion A\" v={`${GEAR.nacelle_sweep}\u00b0 \u00d7 ${GEAR.R_sector}/${GEAR.R_pinion} =
